If we were to gather our authors in one room, it would be the best kind of cocktail party

Our authors’ work spans fiction and nonfiction, adult and children’s, with the distinctions and parallels between them creating a dynamic list.

We see the common ground between our poets and lyricists; musicians and artists; scientists and speculative fiction writers; historians and writers of historical fiction; psychologists and novelists; journalists and activists.

Fiction

We are drawn to fiction that transcends its given category through masterful writing and wide appeal. No matter the genre, we seek multi-faceted characters and relationships we long to revisit and stories that stay with us. We love the funny and quirky, the searing and incisive, the dark and defiant. We thrill at getting lost in other worlds and time periods. We relish contemporary fiction that explores the here and now with startling acuity.

Our authors have won the Pen/Hemingway Award, the World Fantasy Award, the British Fantasy Award, the Aurealis Award, the Locus Award, and the ALA’s Alex Award among numerous other awards, and been finalists for the Hugo, the Nebula, and the Orange Prize.

Our adult novelists include Garth Nix, Jaclyn Moriarty, Melina Marchetta, Alaya Dawn Johnson, Rosina Lippi (also writing as Sara Donati), Delia Sherman, Mary McMyne, Rachel DeWoskin, Sajni Patel, Kara McDowell, Jenny L. Howe, Jesse Q. Sutanto, Lauren Ho, Shauna Robinson, Ellen Klages, and many more.
































Nonfiction

We are drawn to nonfiction authors who are leaders in their field or have lived a singular life, who have something powerful to say, and who distinguish themselves through their writing. Our nonfiction authors include biographers and historians whose narratives compel us like great fiction; scientists who rivet us with their descriptions of the natural world; journalists who bring us deep inside pressing issues through story; memoirists who captivate us with their lives and voice; innovators and entrepreneurs who possess keen insights in business and beyond; and feminists and activists who are advocating for a more equal, more inclusive world.

Our nonfiction authors have won the Pulitzer, the National Book Award, the Michael Lynton History Prize, the Pushcart Prize, the American Book Award, and the Emmy, and have been finalists for the LA Times Book Prize and the Pulitzer, among numerous other awards. They have been the subject of feature film and documentary, and frequently appear on NPR, CNN, MSNBC, TODAY, The Daily Show and other national media. They have been included in anthologies such as Best American Essays and publish in a wide range of publications including: The New York Times, The Atlantic, New York Review of Books, Mother Jones, HuffPost, Elle, O Magazine, Wall Street Journal, Time, The Believer, Lapham’s Quarterly, Boston Globe, Atlas Obscura, The Washington Post, Politico, Chronicle of Higher Education, Orion, Vanity Fair, Harper’s, The Nation, NPR, The Economist, The Forward, The New Yorker, and Sunday Times Magazine London.

Our nonfiction authors include T.J. Stiles, Tyler Anbinder, Leah Redmond Chang, Mikki Kendall, Danya Ruttenberg, Colin Woodard, Alissa Quart, David Rose, Tami Oldham Ashcraft, Polo Tate, Henry Gee, Susanne Antonetta, Eliza Factor, Tiffany Pham, Leil Lowndes, Sally Susman, Melanie Katzman, Sara Jane Ho, and Tamsen Fadal among others.








































Young Adult

Our Young Adult list of fiction and select nonfiction is diverse and dynamic—defined by original voices and stories that challenge, excite, and inspire, and a range of formats including graphic novels and other illustrated works.

We adore contemporaries about love, friendship, and family that feel both timely and timeless; character-driven novels that explore relevant issues and struggles from a young adult perspective; thrillers and mysteries that make us second-guess ourselves; historical fiction that illuminates under-explored time periods; fantasy and science fiction that transports us to entirely new worlds or puts a compelling speculative twist on our own; and nonfiction narratives, memoir, journalism and prescriptive “how to” books of special interest to young adults. Above all, we are drawn to exemplary writing and seek to build a list that reflects the extraordinary diversity of young people’s lives and experiences.

Our Young Adult authors have won the Printz Award, multiple Printz Honors, the National Jewish Book Award, and the Sydney Taylor Book Award. They have been longlisted for the National Book Award and the Miles Franklin Award, and have been finalists for the Edgar Award, the Morris Award, the American Book Award, and the Nebula Award among many others.

Our Young Adult authors include Marissa Meyer, Garth Nix, Scott Westerfeld, S. Jae-Jones, Nafiza Azad, Alaya Dawn Johnson, Jaclyn Moriarty, Melina Marchetta, Margo Lanagan, Justine Larbalestier, Alison Goodman, Julie Chibbaro, Jesse Q. Sutanto, Kara McDowell, Britney S. Lewis, Lyndall Clipstone, Sajni Patel, and many more.






























Children’s

In children’s books, we are drawn to the fun, the fantastical, the heartwarming, and the thought-provoking; and a range of formats, including novels, graphic novels/comics, and picture books.

We seek fast-paced adventures threaded with mystery; voice-and-character driven contemporary stories tackling an important topic from a unique perspective; fantasy and historical that’s whimsical, entertaining, and smart, with heart; and original nonfiction that newly illuminates a fascinating subject through narrative. We love to laugh, and we enjoy the very funny and the delightfully strange, while being equally drawn to serious subject matter and content.

Our children’s authors have won five Newbery Honor Awards. They have also won the Scott O’Dell Award for Historical Fiction, the New York Historical Society Children’s History Book Prize, the Boston Globe Horn Book Honor Award, the Aurealis Award, the Sid Fleischman Award, the National Jewish Book Award, the Grammy Award, and have been finalists for many more honors.

Our children’s list includes John Marciano, Catherine Gilbert Murdock, Kirby Larson, Jennifer and Matthew Holm, Andy Griffiths, Jesse Q Sutanto, Barbara Dee,  Christopher Healy, Delia Sherman, the Estate of Ludwig Bemelmans (Madeline), Laurie Berkner, Alan Silberberg, Billy Sharff, Nora Raleigh Baskin, Shivaun Plozza, Kiyash Monsef, They Might Be Giants, among many other authors and illustrators.
































Authors

Beth Ain

Go to Author Page

Beth Ain is an author of fiction for all ages, including the Starring Jules chapter book series, and two middle grade novels in verse: Izzy Kline Has Butterflies and The Cure for Cold Feet. Her writing has appeared in Scary Mommy, Kveller, and Dame Magazine, and she has taught Creative Writing at Hofstra University. She is currently working on her first novel for adults. She lives in Port Washington, New York with her husband and two children.

Hanna Alkaf

Go to Author Page

Hanna Alkaf is an internationally bestselling, critically acclaimed author of books for kids and teens, including The Weight of Our Sky, The Girl and the Ghost, Queen of the Tiles, Hamra & the Jungle of Memories, and The Hysterical Girls of St. Bernadette’s. She is also co-editor of the YA anthology The Grimoire of Grave Fates, co-creator and author of the MG horror series Tales from Cabin 23, and editor of the upcoming MG Southeast Asian fantasy anthology The Beasts Beneath the Winds. Hanna graduated with a degree in journalism from Northwestern University and is a Freeman Award winner and a Kirkus Prize finalist. She lives in Kuala Lumpur with her family.

TJ Alexander

Go to Author Page

TJ Alexander is an amateur baker and author who writes about queer joy. They are the author of the contemporary romances Chef’s Kiss, Chef’s Choice, Second Chances in New Port Stephen, Triple Sec, and Love & Lake Monsters; and the Regency romances A Gentleman’s Gentleman and A Lady for All Seasons. Their books have been USA Today bestsellers, received multiple starred reviews, been named LAMBDA Literary Award Finalists and Best Books of the Year by TIME and Library Journal, and selected as Amazon Editors’ Picks and Indie Next Picks. Originally from South Florida, they received their MA in Writing & Publishing from Emerson College in Boston. They live in New York City with their wife.

Jacob Anbinder

Go to Author Page

Jacob Anbinder is a historian of the modern United States with a particular focus on cities and suburbs in the twentieth century. His research interests include American politics and political culture, social inequality, housing policy, and infrastructure. Jacob’s writing has been published in The Atlantic, The Wall Street Journal, U.S. News & World Report, Business History Review, The Week, Democracy: A Journal of Ideas, and other outlets. Currently a Klarman Postdoctoral Fellow at Cornell University, he received his PhD in history from Harvard University in 2023. His debut work of political nonfiction, NIMBY Nation, will be published by Bloomsbury in 2027.

Tyler Anbinder

Go to Author Page

Tyler Anbinder is a retired professor of history and former chair of the History Department at George Washington University, and author of three award-winning books of historical nonfiction: Nativism and Slavery, winner of the Avery Craven Prize of the Organization of American Historians; Five Points, winner of the New York City Book Prize of 2001; and City of Dreams, winner of the Mark Lynton Prize for History. His latest book, Plentiful Country: The Great Potato Famine and the Making of Irish New York, was published by Little, Brown and Company in March 2024 . He also served as a consultant to Martin Scorsese for the Academy Award-nominated film Gangs of New York.

Becca Andrews

Go to Author Page

Becca Andrews is the author of No Choice, based on her Mother Jones cover story about the past, present, and future of Roe v. Wade, published by PublicAffairs in October 2022. She is a graduate of UC Berkeley’s School of Journalism and wrote for newspapers in her home state of Tennessee.

Craig Archibald

Go to Author Page

Craig Archibald is the founder of The Archibald Studio in Los Angeles, where he coaches actors, writers, and directors. His clients include Constance Wu, Katherine Erbe, Beth Behrs, Eric Roberts, and Dan Futterman. He has guided his clients to wins and nominations for numerous awards, including the Golden Globes, SAG Awards, Oscars, and Emmys. He is an actor himself and appeared as Christopher Isherwood in the Academy Award-winning film Capote. He is also a playwright, screenwriter, and short film director and producer. His first book, The Actor’s Mindset: Acting as a Craft, Discipline and Business, was published by Applause Books. He has a B.A. from the University of Saskatchewan and graduated from the Neighborhood Playhouse in New York City, where he studied with Sanford Meisner.

Tami Oldham Ashcraft

Go to Author Page

Tami Oldham Ashcraft is the author, with Susea McGearhart, of the New York Times-bestselling memoir Adrift: A True Story of Love, Loss, and Survival at Sea (originally published as Red Sky in Mourning) which was adapted into the 2018 film of the same name starring Shailene Woodley and Sam Claflin. It is also an international bestseller and has been published in over 14 foreign territories. Tami continues to be an avid sailor and is a 100-ton licensed captain with over 50,000 offshore miles. She lives in Friday Harbor, Washington.

Alex Aster

Go to Author Page

Alex Aster is the #1 New York Times bestselling author of Lightlark, which was published by Abrams in August 2022 and sold into over a dozen foreign territories, and its sequel, Nightbane. She is also the author of the award-winning middle grade novels Curse of the Night Witch and Curse of the Forgotten City, inspired by Latinx myths from her childhood. Curse of the Night Witch was named one of the Best Books of 2020 by Kirkus and Amazon, was on Good Morning America, and received multiple starred reviews. She graduated summa cum laude from the University of Pennsylvania, where she studied English, and now lives in New York.

Nafiza Azad

Go to Author Page

Nafiza Azad is a young adult author. She is the author of The Candle and the Flame, a 2020 William C. Morris YA Debut Award finalist, The Wild Ones, and Road of the Lost. She co-edited the anthology Writing in Color: The Lessons We’ve Learned with Melody Simpson, and will co-edit A Thousand Nights with Intisar Khanani, a new anthology forthcoming from Candlewick in 2026. Her writing can be found in the anthologies Come On In, Every Body Shines, and Magic Has No Borders. She holds a Master of Arts degree in Children’s Literature from the University of British Columbia. Born in Fiji, she currently lives in British Columbia, Canada, with her family.

Taryn Bashford

Go to Author Page

Taryn Bashford is an author of contemporary young adult novels, including The Harper Effect. Before becoming a full-time writer, she received a degree in English from Exeter University, worked in advertising, and was the CEO of a London-based internet company. She grew up in England, and currently lives in Queensland, Australia with her family.

Nora Raleigh Baskin

Go to Author Page

Nora Raleigh Baskin is the award-winning author of books for children and teens, including Anything But Typical (winner of the ALA Schneider Family Book Award), What Every Girl (Except Me) Knows, The Truth About My Bat Mitzvah, The Summer Before Boys, Ruby on the Outside, and Nine, Ten: A September 11 Story. Her work also appeared in the anthology Coming of Age: 13 B’nai Mitzvah Stories. She is the co-author of Seven Clues to Home (Knopf, 2020) and Consider the Octopus (Holt, 2022) with Gae Polisner, and The Planet, the Portal and a Pizza (Little Brown, 2025), with Wendy Mass.. She lives with her family in Connecticut.

Jennifer Baumgardner

Go to Author Page

Jennifer Baumgardner is a journalist, filmmaker, lecturer, and author of numerous books about contemporary feminism, including Manifesta: Young Women, Feminism, and the Future and Grassroots: A Field Guide for Feminist Activism (co-authored with Amy Richards), F’em!: Goo Goo, Gaga, and Some Thoughts on Balls, and Look Both Ways: Bisexual Politics. With Richards, she is also the cofounder of the speaker’s bureau Soapbox Inc, and was formerly Executive Director at The Feminist Press. She is founder and editor-in-chief of Dottir Press, whose mission is passing on and building upon feminist intellectual and creative legacies. She lives in New York City with her husband and two sons.

Estate of Ludwig Bemelmans

Go to Author Page

Ludwig Bemelmans was a painter, illustrator, and writer for both children and adults best known for the Madeline books, which rank among the most-honored and recognizable children’s book series of all time. Madeline was named a Caldecott Honor Book, and its sequel Madeline’s Rescue, won the Caldecott Medal and was a New York Times Outstanding Book of the Year. Bemelmans died in 1962. His grandson continued the Madeline series with the picture books Madeline and the Cats of Rome, Madeline at the White House, and Madeline and the Old House in Paris; the early reader books Madeline’s Tea Party and Madeline and Her Dog, and a forthcoming graphic novel to be illustrated by LeUyen Pham and Alex Puvilland. Viking has also released a series of new picture books featuring Madeline: Love from Madeline, Madeline’s ABCs, Madeline’s 123s, and Madeline’s Seasons. Pushkin Press reissued Bemelmans’ beloved adult nonfiction titles Hotel Splendide and To the One I Love the Best in 2022, and will release How to Travel Incognito in 2026.

Laurie Berkner

Go to Author Page

Laurie Berkner is a singer, songwriter, lyricist, and author, recognized as “the queen of children’s music” by her devoted fans. The founder of her own label, Two Tomatoes Records, she has released eleven bestselling, award-winning albums and frequently appears in concert and on television specials. She is the author of five picture books, including We Are The Dinosaurs, Pillowland, and Monster Boogie.

Tamar Bihari

Go to Author Page

Tamar Bihari is an award-winning romance author and screenwriter. Under a pseudonym, she has won the prestigious Romance Writers of America’s Golden Heart Award, and was nominated for a RITA award. Several of her screenplays have been optioned by producers. She currently lives with her family in Queens.

Remica Bingham-Risher

Go to Author Page

Remica Bingham-Risher is a poet, interviewer and essayist, a Cave Canem fellow and Affrilachian Poet. Her book, Soul Culture: Black Poets, Books, and Questions That Grew Me Up, was published by Beacon Press in September 2022. Her work has been featured in The New York Times, The Writer’s Chronicle, Callaloo, Essence and a host of other outlets. She is the author of Conversion, winner of the Naomi Long Madgett Poetry Award; What We Ask of Flesh, shortlisted for the Hurston/Wright Legacy Award and Starlight & Error, winner of the Diode Editions Book Award and a finalist for the Library of Virginia Book Award. Her next poetry collection, Room Swept Home, was published by Wesleyan University Press in February 2024. She is the Director of Quality Enhancement Plan Initiatives, which help faculty integrate writing into the classroom, at Old Dominion University. She resides in Norfolk, Virginia with her husband and children.

Chesa Boudin

Go to Author Page

​Chesa Boudin was the 29th District Attorney for the City and County of San​ ​Francisco. Prior to his election in 2019, he served as a Deputy​ ​Public Defender where he tried dozens of cases to verdict and led the​ ​office’s efforts on bail reform. His work won the National Association​ ​of Criminal Defense Lawyers 2019 National Champion of Justice Award. A​ ​Rhodes Scholar and a graduate of Yale College and Yale Law School,​ ​Chesa served as a law clerk to two federal judges. Chesa has translated, edited, and authored several books. His scholarly law articles cover a range of topics such as direct democracy, immigration, institution building, the rights of children with incarcerated parents, and prison visitation policies. Chesa lives in San Francisco with his wife and enjoys surfing in his spare time.  

K. Tempest Bradford

Go to Author Page

K. Tempest Bradford is a science fiction and fantasy writer, writing instructor, media critic, reviewer, and podcaster. Her debut middle grade novel, Ruby Finley vs. the Interstellar Invasion was published by FSG Children’s and won the Nebula’s Andre Norton Award for Middle Grade and Young Adult Fiction. Her short fiction has appeared in multiple anthologies and magazines including Strange Horizons, PodCastle, Sunspot Jungle, In the Shadow of the Towers, and many more. She’s the host of ORIGINality, a podcast about the roots of creative genius, and contributes to several more. Her media criticism and reviews can be found on NPR, io9, and in books about Time Lords. When not writing, she teaches classes on writing inclusive fiction through LitReactor and Writing the Other.com.

Krystle Brantzeg

Go to Author Page

In fifth grade Krystle Brantzeg won an essay contest and still has the prized polyester jacket to prove it. Long after that win, she received her BA in literature and writing and worked as a writer and graphic designer for a wedding magazine. She now authors stories and adventures with her husband, quartet of kids, and brassy rescue terrier, who make her feel like a winner every day. Her debut novel, Indoor Girl, will be published by Scholastic in 2027.

Elaine Meryl Brown, Marsha Haygood & Rhonda Joy McLean

Go to Author Page

Elaine Meryl Brown, Marsha Haygood, and Rhonda Joy McLean are the authors of The Little Black Book of Success: Laws of Leadership for Black Women — which was a national bestseller and NAACP Literary Award nominee — and the companion The Little Black Book of Success Workbook. Elaine Meryl Brown is the former vice president, special markets and Cinemax Group, at HBO. Prior to joining HBO, she was a creative director at Showtime Networks. An Emmy Award–winning writer and the author of the novels Lemon City and Playing by the Rules, she is currently writing her third novel and is based in New Jersey. Marsha Haygood is the President and Founder of StepWise Associates, LLC.  She is a Leadership and Personal Development Consultant and was formerly the Executive Vice President of human resources and administration at New Line Cinema and Orion Pictures. She lives in New York and Florida. Rhonda Joy McLean is the President and CEO of RJMLEADS LLC, a leadership consulting and career advancement company. She is the former deputy general counsel of Time Inc. and the former assistant regional director of the Northeast Region of the Federal Trade Commission. She has worked as a corporate litigator in a major New York law firm and taught administrative law at the City University of New York. She lives in New York City.

Peter Bunzl

Go to Author Page

Peter Bunzl is an author, animator, screenwriter, and director who has worked on numerous commercials, music videos, and two BAFTA-winning children’s TV series. He is the author of The Cogheart Adventures, a middle grade fantasy series — comprised of Cogheart, Moonlocket, and Sky Circus — that has been nominated for the Waterstones’ Children’s Book Prize and the Carnegie Medal. He lives with his partner in North London.

Gemma Burgess

Go to Author Page

Gemma Burgess is an award-winning screenwriter and author. As a novelist, she is the author of The Dating Detox, A Girl Like You, and The Brooklyn Girls series of new adult novels, and has been published in over twenty foreign territories. She is the show-runner, co-executive producer, and writer for My Lady Jane, an adaptation of the novel of the same name, which aired on Amazon Prime in 2024. She has sold screenplays, television pilots and specs to New Regency, ABC, NBC, ABC Signature, Bravo, Amazon, and Universal. Her half-hour comedy spec Filthy Liar was awarded “Best Comedy” in the WGA Made in NY screenwriting competition, and subsequently optioned by Hat Trick. She lives in London with her family.

Matt Burns

Go to Author Page

Matt Burns is an animation producer and writer of books for kids and teens. His debut young adult novel, Smooth, was published by Candlewick Press. Born in Georgia, he currently lives in Brooklyn, New York. 

Sasha Cagen

Go to Author Page

Sasha Cagen is a writer, coach, and founder of the quirkyalone movement, about supporting women and men who prefer to be single rather than settle, with special focus on the empowerment of women to know their worth. Her work has been featured in dozens of media outlets including CNN, NPR, BBC, The New York Times, USA Today, and The Wall Street Journal. She is the author of Quirkyalone: A Manifesto for Uncompromising Romantics, To-Do List: From Buying Milk to Finding a Soul Mate, What Our Lists Reveal About Us, and is at work on a memoir. She spent five years in Buenos Aires, Argentina teaching, where she created the Tango Adventure, an immersive course that combines dance lessons with life coaching, and is currently based in her native Rhode Island.

Sam Cameron

Go to Author Page

Sam Cameron is a Formula 1 fan and writer of queer love stories. She put these two passions​ together, and now she writes queer F1 rom coms based on Shakespeare plays. After ten years as​ a high school history teacher, Sam shifted gears to become a book coach and freelance editor.​ She is the author of the weekly Substack Truant Pen where she shares actionable advice for stuck​ writers.

Leah Redmond Chang

Go to Author Page

Leah Redmond Chang is a former Associate Professor of French and Director of the French Literature Program at George Washington University, and was most recently a Senior Research Associate at University College London. She is the author of Young Queens, about the intertwined stories of four Renaissance-era queens (Catherine de’ Medici, Elizabeth Tudor, Mary Stuart, and Elisabeth de Valois), was published by FSG and Bloomsbury UK, and has been translated into five foreign languages. It was longlisted for the inaugural 2024 Women’s Prize for Nonfiction and was a finalist for the 2023 Los Angeles Times Book Prize for Biography. Her next book, The Nuns, will be published by Avid Reader Press and Bloomsbury UK. She is also the author of two scholarly works: Into Print: The Production of Female Authorship in Early Modern France and Portraits of the Queen Mother: Polemics, Panegyrics, Letters, winner of the Josephine Roberts Award from the International Society for the Study of Early Modern Women. She lives with her husband and three children in Washington, DC and London.

Julie Chibbaro

Go to Author Page

Julie Chibbaro is the author of three novels for teens: Into the Dangerous World, a Junior Library Guild Selection that was nominated for numerous state awards; Deadly, winner of the National Jewish Book Award and a Bank Street Best Book; and Redemption, winner of the American Book Award. She has taught workshops for the Gotham Writers Workshop and The Highlights Foundation, and has written for The Prague Post, The Montreal Gazette, Hudson Valley Magazine, and many other outlets. She lives with her family in Beacon, New York, where she founded Get Lit Beacon, a reading series and literary salon.

Samantha Chong

Go to Author Page

Samantha Chong is a Malaysian fiction writer currently based in New York City. Her debut YA novel, Prodigal Tiger was published by Putnam Children’s. Having lived all around the globe, Samantha is interested in exploring what it means to belong, whether that’s tied to places, people, or the myriad of ways that someone can carve out their space in an increasingly shifting world. When she’s not writing, Samantha is out and about exploring, keeping an eye out for the hidden histories waiting to be shared, or in her kitchen, attempting to whip up culinary creations.

J K Chukwu

Go to Author Page

J K Chukwu is a writer and visual artist from the Midwest. Her debut novel, The Unfortunates was published Mariner Books in February 2023, and was a GMA Buzz Pick. She also contributed to Against Enough, a non-fiction collection exploring Nigeria through its writers, released by The Borough Press in the UK, and the forthcoming horror collection Of The Flesh. She holds an MFA in Literary Arts from Brown University and a BA from the University of Chicago. She was a 2019 Lambda Fellow and her work has appeared in Black Warrior Review, DIAGRAM, and TAYO. She has presented her writing and art at University of Wisconsin-Madison, National Louis University, The University of Manchester, and elsewhere.

Lyndall Clipstone

Go to Author Page

Lyndall Clipstone is the author of the World at the Lake’s Edge duology — comprised of Lakesedge and Forestfall — and Unholy Terrors, a 2023 Indie Next Pick. Her latest novel, Tenderly, I am Devoured, was published simultaneously in the US, UK, and Australia in July 2025. Her short fiction has been published in the anthologies Crawling Moon and Blood, Sweat, and Queers. A former youth librarian, she has a Bachelors in Creative Writing and a Graduate Diploma in Library and Information Management. She has participated as both a mentee and mentor in Author Mentor Match and lives in Adelaide, South Australia with her partner and two sons.

Melissa Coffey

Go to Author Page

Melissa Coffey is the author of Fridge-opolis, her debut picture book which won the 2022 Writers’ League of Texas Discovery Award, and the forthcoming One Spark (2026). She spent 20+ years writing professionally for grown-ups before discovering her passion writing for kids. She loves creating stories that garner giggles, expand horizons and inspire action and emotion. Melissa lives with her family in Austin, Texas.  

Candice Marley Conner

Go to Author Page

Candice Marley Conner is a children’s book author and Local Liaison for the Society of Children’s Book Writers and Illustrators. She is currently working on a middle grade novel. She is the author of the YA novel, The Existence of Bea Pearl, published by Owl Hollow Press, and two picture books: Sassafras and Her Teeny Tiny Tail and Chompsey Chomps Books, both released by MacLaren-Cochrane Publishing. She lives with her family in Alabama.

Desiree Cooper

Go to Author Page

Desiree Cooper is a 2015 Kresge Artist Fellow, former attorney, and Pulitzer Prize-nominated journalist who writes extensively about racial and gender equality. Her debut collection of flash fiction, Know the Mother, won numerous awards, including 2017 Next Generation Indie Book Award. Cooper’s fiction, poetry and essays have appeared in The Best Small Fictions 2018, Callaloo, Michigan Quarterly Review, The Rumpus, and River Teeth, among other publications. Her essay “We Have Lost Too Many Wigs” was listed as a notable essay in The Best American Essays 2019, and her essay “What If I Hadn’t Been There to Catch Them?” ran in the New York Times‘ Modern Love column in 2023. She is the author of the children’s book Nothing Special, and editor of the anthology Black Summers: Detroiters Take on the Great Outdoors, both published by Wayne State University Press. After spending her thirty-year career in Detroit, she now lives in the Virginia Beach area, where she cares for her mother and three grandchildren.

Codie Crowley

Go to Author Page

Codie Crowley told a scary story at a childhood slumber party that caused a mass hysteria, and she’s been chasing that high ever since. When she isn’t writing chilling fiction, she’s probably out looking for ghosts in Salem, Massachusetts, where she lives with her husband, their cat, and a collection of creepy dolls. She is the USA Today-bestselling author of Here Lies a Vengeful Bitch and Body Count, published by Disney-Hyperion.

Belinda Dai

Go to Author Page

Belinda Dai is a Pushcart Prize-nominated writer whose short fiction has been published in the U.S. in Pigeon Pages NYC, Pithead Chapel, Split Lip and elsewhere. Her short fiction and visual arts reviews are also widely published in Australia, where she has been involved in the literary sector through various programs and festivals. 

Cody Luke Davis

Go to Author Page

Cody Luke Davis is a writer whose debut psychological thriller, House of a Thousand Lies, was published by Crooked Lane Books in 2022. He previously worked in books-to-film at CAA and The Story Factory. He lives in his native Los Angeles with his wife and two rescue pitbulls.

Barbara Dee

Go to Author Page

Barbara Dee is the author of over a dozen highly acclaimed middle-grade novels, including Tear This Down, Unstuck, Haven Jacobs Saves the Planet, Violets are Blue, My Life in the Fish Tank, Maybe He Just Likes You, Everything I Know About You, Star-Crossed, and Halfway Normal. Her next two books, Nothing to See Here and Your Eyes Are Like Two Clouds, are forthcoming from Scholastic in 2026 and 2027 respectively. Her books frequently appear on “best of” lists and have received numerous starred reviews. She has authored viral blog posts about inclusivity in children’s book publishing, and frequently appears at schools, conferences, and in media outlets to discuss her work and the handling of sensitive topics in middle grade fiction. She is a co-founder of the annual Chappaqua Children’s Book Festival, and lives in Westchester County, New York with her family.

Katelyn Detweiler

Go to Author Page

Katelyn Detweiler is a young adult author and literary agent. She received her BA in English Literature from Penn State University and previously worked in the marketing department at Macmillan Children’s Group before joining Jill Grinberg Literary Management as an agent. Her YA novels include the duology Immaculate and Transcendent, The Undoing of Thistle Tate, and The People We Choose. She also writes romantic comedies for adults as Kate Tamberelli. She lives with her husband and children in Brooklyn, New York.

Tad Devine

Go to Author Page

Tad Devine is a Democratic political strategist with over thirty years of campaign experience. Prior to serving as chief strategist for Bernie Sanders’s 2016 presidential campaign, he was a senior adviser in Al Gore’s 2000 and John Kerry’s 2004 presidential campaigns. In addition to his political work and media consulting, he has held academic positions at Boston University, the Harvard Kennedy School, and New York University, among others. His book, How the Democrats Screwed Bernie, will be published by Avid Reader Press in July 2026.

Rachel DeWoskin

Go to Author Page

Rachel DeWoskin is the author of six books of fiction and nonfiction for teens and adults: Foreign Babes in Beijing: Behind the Scenes of a New China, Repeat After Me, Big Girl Small (winner of ALA’s Alex Award), Blind, Someday We Will Fly (winner of the National Jewish Book Award and ALA’s Sydney Taylor Book Award), and Banshee. She is also an accomplished poet, and has published two volumes of poetry with University of Chicago Press: Two Menus and absolute animal. Her essays and articles have appeared in The New Yorker, Vanity Fair, The Sunday Times Magazine of London, Teachers and Writers, and numerous anthologies. She spent her twenties in China as the unlikely star of a nighttime soap opera, which became the basis for her memoir. She has been a professor of fiction, creative nonfiction, and poetry at New York University, Boston University, and the University of Chicago. She is the curator of Theater for One’s Poetry for One project, a resident poet at the Goodman Theater, a member of the national steering committee of Writers for Democratic Action (WDA), a professor for Illinois’ Prison Neighborhood Arts Project, and teaches at Stateville Prison. She lives in Chicago with her husband and two daughters.

Alda P. Dobbs

Go to Author Page

Alda P. Dobbs is the author of the historical novels Barefoot Dreams of Petra Luna and its follow-up, The Other Side of the River. Petra Luna was nominated for the Texas Bluebonnet Master List Award and received a Pura Belpre Honor Award, and The Other Side of the River won a Northern Lights Book Award for Middle Grade Historical Fiction. Her latest book, The Giving Flower, a picture book exploring the story of the Poinsettia, was published by Sourcebooks. Alda was born in a small town in northern Mexico but moved to San Antonio, Texas as a child. She studied physics and worked as an engineer before pursuing her love of storytelling. She’s as passionate about connecting children to their past, their communities, different cultures and nature as she is about writing. Alda lives with her husband and two children outside Houston, Texas.

Catharina Doria

Go to Author Page


Catharina Doria is an AI ethicist, consultant, and content creator with over 180,000 followers across social platforms. In 2023, she was recognized as one of the 100 most brilliant women in ethical AI for her work in AI literacy—empowering people to understand artificial intelligence and navigate the challenges of today’s tech world. Her work blends technology, culture, and responsibility, making complex ideas accessible and engaging. She produces bilingual content (Portuguese and English) that resonates with global audiences and is guided by her motto: “making learning cool again.” Catharina holds a bachelor’s degree in Communication and Digital Media, and a master’s in Business Analytics and Big Data from IE University, both with honors. With a distinctive voice and a forward-looking perspective, she is a trusted guide for those seeking to innovate with purpose and think beyond the present. Catharina lives in São Paulo, Brazil, with her dog Pipoca (Popcorn), and loves playing beach tennis with friends.

Alechia Dow

Go to Author Page

Alechia Dow is a former pastry chef, librarian, author of YA sci-fi-fantasies: The Sound of Stars (a Junior Library Guild Selection and Hal Clement ALA notable), The Kindred (Indie Next Kids Jan 2022 Pick), A Song of Salvation, Until the Clock Strikes Midnight, several short anthology pieces, and the middle grade novels Just a Pinch of Magic and The Cookie Crumbles, which she co-authored with Tracy Badua. When not writing, you can find her having epic dance parties with her daughter, baking, reading, taking teeny adventures, and exploring her local food scene.

Zoé Duhaime

Go to Author Page

Zoé Duhaime is a writer living in Montreal. Her debut novel, The Lost Spectacular will be published by Erewhon in the US and Solaris in the UK in September 2026.

Andrea Eames

Go to Author Page

Andrea Eames is an English poet and Sunday Times bestselling novelist now based in Texas after eight years in New Zealand and seventeen years in Zimbabwe. She is the author of A Harvest of Hearts and A Tangled Magic, published by Erewhon Books and Voyager UK. Her first two novels, published by Penguin Random House UK, were set in Zimbabwe, where she grew up: The Cry of the Go-Away Bird (2011) and The White Shadow (2012), which was shortlisted for the Dylan Thomas Prize in 2012. She holds an MFA in Creative Writing, and works part-time as a fairy at the Renaissance Faire. Andrea lives in the country with her husband, baby daughter, and five very ungrateful cats.

L.M. Elliott

Go to Author Page

L.M. Elliott was an award-winning magazine journalist before becoming a New York Times best-selling writer of young adult historical and biographical fiction. She has published over a dozen titles, exploring a variety of topics and eras—WWII, the American Revolution, the Cold War, McCarthyism, and Renaissance Italy. They include Under a War-Torn Sky and A Troubled Peace, Hamilton and Peggy!, Da Vinci’s Tiger, Suspect Red, Storm Dog, Walls, Louisa June and the Nazis in the Waves, and Bea and the New Deal Horse (recipient of the 2024 Scott O’Dell Award for Historical Fiction). Her next book is Truth, Lies, and the Questions in Between (Algonquin Young Readers, 2025). Her books have received awards from the Bank Street College of Education Best Books, NCSS/CBC Notables, Jefferson Cup Honor Books, Grateful American Awards, and have been finalists on numerous state award lists.

Eliza Factor

Go to Author Page

Eliza Factor is the author of two novels, Love Maps and The Mercury Fountain, and the memoir Strange Beauty: A Portrait of My Son. She is the founder of Extreme Kids & Crew, a nonprofit community center that connects families raising children with disabilities through the arts and play. She lives with her family in Brooklyn, New York and Dublin, New Hampshire.

Tamsen Fadal

Go to Author Page

Tamsen Fadal is an award-winning journalist, menopause advocate, and social media’s ‘midlife mentor.’ After more than three decades as a news anchor, Tamsen made the bold decision to pivot her career towards women’s health advocacy. She’s the best friend helping to guide women through midlife and beyond, acting as a one-stop resource for everything they need to know to live their best lives. She has established herself as a leading voice in the menopause space with a passionate, loyal, social media audience of 3+ million. Her latest book, How to Menopause was published in March 2025 by GCP Balance and was an instant New York Times, IndieBound, and USA Today bestseller. Her regular appearances in media outlets include Oprah Daily, “The Today Show,” The New York Times, and The Harvard Business Review. Tamsen is the producer and executive producer of the PBS documentary The M Factor: Shredding the Silence on Menopause. From the boardroom to the stage at SXSW to Capitol Hill, she advocates for policies that eliminate the stigma of menopause and foster a work environment where women feel comfortable discussing and managing their midlife health journey. Tamsen holds a certificate in holistic health coaching from the Institute for Integrative Nutrition, and serves on the national board of Let’s Talk Menopause.

Rita Feinstein

Go to Author Page

Rita Feinstein is the author of the young adult novel-in-verse Meet Me in the Fourth Dimension (Page Street, 2024) and two poetry collections from Brain Mill Press. Her work has appeared in Willow Springs, Salamander, and Beloit Poetry Journal, among other publications, and has been nominated for Best of the Net and Best New Poets. She received her MFA from Oregon State University and now lives in Washington, DC with her husband, who is a lawyer, and her dog, who is not.

Jessica Ferri

Go to Author Page

Jessica Ferri is a freelance writer whose work has been published by The New Yorker’s Page Turner Blog, The Economist, NPR, Yahoo!, Bustle, Time Out New York, The Lineup, The Barnes and Noble Review, and The Daily Beast, where she is a regular contributor. Her books, Silent Cities New York and Silent Cities San Francisco, began as an Instagram travelogue chronicling cemeteries and their unique and largely forgotten stories. She lives with her family in the Bay Area.

Bex Tobin Fine

Go to Author Page

Bex Tobin Fine is a writer of two picture books: You Are Home (Chronicle, April 2025) and Floor It! (Random House, September 2025). She was a solo violinist for many years, playing with countless orchestras, including the Toronto Symphony Orchestra, Windsor Symphony Orchestra, and McGill Chamber Orchestra. She was also an award-winning on-camera and voice actor, voicing lead roles in numerous children’s animated shows, such as Rolie Polie Olie and Miss Spider’s Sunny Patch Friends. She was a counselor in various settings as well as a high school English, social science, and sciences teacher. She began focusing on writing after getting her master’s degree from the University of Toronto. She now spends her days writing, drinking too much tea, and exploring the world near and far with her husband and daughter.

Kylie Flanagan

Go to Author Page

Kylie Flanagan is a climate justice and resilience strategist, educator, author, and director of a small, place-based foundation.  Her first book, Climate Resilience was published by North Atlantic Books in August 2023. She holds a Masters in Sustainability Solutions from Presidio Graduate School and a Bachelor of Arts degree from Dartmouth College, where she studied geography and environmental studies. She lives with her partner in the San Francisco Bay Area.

Jonathan Follett

Go to Author Page

Jonathan Follett is a designer, and music producer as well as the co-author, with Matthew Holm, of the middle grade novel Marvin and the Moths. He and Matt are also co-founders of Hot Knife Design, Inc., and have consulted on projects for the New York Public Library, Hearst Publishing, Random House, and Vodafone. He lives in Massachusetts.

Kate Forsyth

Go to Author Page

Kate Forsyth is the bestselling and award-winning author of more than twenty books, ranging from picture books to poetry to novels for both children and adults, including the Witches of Eileanan series. She is the only author to win five Aurealis Awards in a single year and was voted one of Australia’s Favorite 25 Novelists. Her books have been published in fourteen countries. She completed her doctorate in fairy tale retellings at the University of Technology in Sydney in 2014. Kate lives in Sydney, Australia.

Katalina Gamarra

Go to Author Page

Katalina Gamarra is the author of Ben and Beatriz, a contemporary reimagining of Much Ado About Nothing that was published in August 2022 by Graydon House. She earned a BA in English from Drew University where she received accolades for both creative writing and academic prowess, as well as an award in Shakespeare Study. Her work has been published on Thought Catalog and Verge of Verse and before becoming an author, she worked in bookselling, literary scouting, manuscript reading, and galley critiquing. She lives in Boston.

Henry Gee

Go to Author Page

Henry Gee is a longtime editor at the science journal Nature and author of several works of nonfiction and fiction. His book A (Very) Short History of Life on Earth: 4.6 Billion Years in 12 Pithy Chapters, was published in Fall 2021 by Picador in the UK and by St. Martin’s in the US, has sold in over a two dozen foreign territories, and won the Royal Society Science Book Prize. Picador and St. Martin’s published his latest book The Decline and Fall of the Human Empire in 2025, and Two Hoots, an imprint of Pan Macmillan, released The Wonder of Life on Earth, a young readers’ book illustrated by Raxenne Maniquiz that charts the story of life on our planet, in 2026. His previous books include In Search of Deep Time: Beyond the Fossil Record to a New History of Life, Jacob’s Ladder: The History of the Human Genome, The Accidental Species: Misunderstandings of Human Evolution, Across the Bridge: Understanding Vertebrate Origins, and The Science of Middle Earth; his fiction includes The Sigil trilogy, By the Sea, and Hunting Unicorns. His book A Field Guide to Dinosaurs was illustrated by Luis V. Rey. He lives in Cromer, Norfolk, England, with his family.

Colin Gigl

Go to Author Page

Colin Gigl is the author of the adult novel, The Ferryman Institute, which was published by Gallery Books in September 2016. It received SF/F Book of the Month honors from both Amazon and Barnes & Noble; a starred review from Booklist; and a starred review and Debut of the Month from Library Journal. His debut middle grade novel, Starfarer, will be published by Viking in 2027. He graduated from Trinity College with degrees in English and Computer Science and now works in tech by day. He lives with his amazing wife and three wonderful daughters who are the lights of his life

Salinee Goldenberg

Go to Author Page

Salinee Goldenberg is a speculative fiction writer and multimedia artist who lives in Washington DC, and is drawn to outsider perspectives. She is the author of The Last Phi Hunter and Way of the Walker, published by Angry Robot. A biracial, bisexual, diaspora writer, Sal often explores themes of identity, obsession and alienation in her work. Previously, she worked at Bethesda Softworks creating narrative trailers for games such as Skyrim, Fallout 4 and Dishonored, and now produces videos for Minecraft. When not writing, she likes to paint, listen to records, and play in punk bands.

Lori Goldstein

Go to Author Page

Lori Goldstein is an author of novels for readers of all ages. Her contemporary YA novels Screen Queens and Sources Say were published by Razorbill in 2019 and 2020, respectively. Her debut fantasy Becoming Jinn was published in 2015, followed by a sequel, Circle of Jinn in 2016. As Lori Anne Goldstein, she is the author of Love, Theodosia, a debut historical romance published by Arcade in November 2021. Before becoming a full-time writer, she received her bachelor’s degree in journalism and worked as an editor and graphic designer. She lives in Boston.

Beverly Gooden

Go to Author Page

Beverly Gooden is a social activist and speaker known for her groundbreaking hashtag movement, #WhyIStayed and the author of Surviving: Why We Stay and How We Leave Abusive Relationships, which won the 2023 Nautilus Book Award in the Memoir and Personal Journey category. Her writing has appeared in the New York Times, the U.S. Office on Women’s Health, and TIME Magazine. She is the recipient of Investigation Discovery and Glamour magazine’s Inspire A Difference “Everyday Hero” Award and was featured in a Toyota commercial discussing her work with survivors of intimate partner violence. Beverly holds a bachelor’s degree in communications and a master’s degree in social justice. She lives in Houston, TX with her many cactus plants.

Alison Goodman

Go to Author Page

Alison Goodman is the acclaimed author of numerous books of young adult fantasy, including Singing the Dogstar Blues, the New York Times bestselling duology Eon and Eona, and the multi-award winning Lady Helen trilogy, comprising The Dark Days Club, The Dark Days Pact, and The Dark Days Deceit. She has published two novels for adults with Berkley, The Benevolent Society of Ill-Mannered Ladies and The Ladies Road Guide to Utter Ruin.. She lives with her husband in Australia, and recently completed her PhD at the University of Queensland.

Hayden Goodman

Go to Author Page Go to Portfolio

Hayden Goodman is an African-American painter, illustrator, and character artist born and raised in the wilderness of Western Massachusetts. She holds a degree in Illustration from The School of Visual Arts and previously worked in animation. Her debut picture book, The Green Piano by Grammy Award-winner Roberta Flack and Tonya Bolden, was published in January 2023 by Anne Schwartz Books. Her forthcoming illustration projects include The Heart Never Forgets by Ana Ot (Atheneum, 2024), The Night Before Christmas (Holt, 2025), a visual reimagining of Clement Clarke Moore’s classic poem, Imagine New Suns by Gwendolyn Wallace (S&S, 2026), a picture book biography of Octavia Butler, and Fangs, Feathers, and Folklore by B. Sharise Moore, a middle grade field guide to mythological African beasts. Hayden’s editorial illustrations have been published in The New Yorker and the New York Times. She works primarily in gouache, painting the witches, goblins and other mysterious creatures from the folklore of her childhood.

b.t. gottfred

Go to Author Page

b.t. gottfred is a screenwriter, producer, director, playwright, and young adult novelist. His series of interconnected contemporary novels include Forever for a Year, The Nerdy and the Dirty (chosen by BookRiot as one of 50 Must-Read YA Novels of the past decade), and The Handsome Girl and Her Beautiful Boy. As a playwright, his work is critically-acclaimed and has had runs in Los Angeles, Chicago and off-Broadway in New York. He has written, directed, and produced numerous digital series and feature films. With Corey Moss, he is the co-founder of the production company Bold Soul Studios. He lives in Los Angeles with his family.

Andy Griffiths

Go to Author Page

Andy Griffiths is Australia’s most popular and bestselling children’s author. With illustrator Terry Denton, he has published more than thirty bestselling books since 1997, including the acclaimed and New York Times bestselling Treehouse series. Their books have sold over ten million copies globally, have received dozens of children’s choice awards, and have been published in over 35 foreign territories. His latest works are You and Me and the Land of Lost Things and You and Me and the Peanut Butter Beast, the first two installments of a new series illustrated by Bill Hope.

Taylor Grothe

Go to Author Page

Taylor Grothe (none/they/she) is a queer, Autistic writer of YA and adult horror. Their YA novels, Hollow and Of Auras and Shadows, are published by Peachtree Teen. Shorts of theirs can be found in Coffin Bell Journal, Shortwave Magazine, This is Tense by Bag of Bones Press, and Haven Speculative. They acted as the 2022–2023 graduate assistant managing editor of Brevity and they are an MFA student at Fairfield University. In their free time, Taylor slowly kills their family’s many houseplants and lives to serve their black cat, Hekla, in the most haunted state in America. 

Charlotte Gunnufson

Go to Author Page

Charlotte Gunnufson is the author of several picture books, including Prince and Pirate (which received a starred review from Kirkus), Halloween Hustle, Dream Submarine (Candlewick, 2024), and Hard Hat Hank and the Sky-High Solution (Planet Possible, 2025). Her forthcoming picture books include School Spirits (Atheneum, 2026). Her poems have appeared in the Cricket and Highlights family of magazines and she is a four-time recipient of Highlights Pewter Plate award. Charlotte holds a bachelor’s degree in English and a Master’s of Arts degree in Teaching.

Elnora Gunter

Go to Author Page

Elnora Gunter is an author of contemporary, contemporary romance, contemporary fantasy, horror, and speculative fiction mainly in the young adult space but also writes select genres in adult and short stories. No matter the age group or label, her stories will always include love, family dynamics, the weight of decision-making and a thread of hope. Her short stories “Food for the Soul” was published in FIYAH Literary Magazine (Issue 23: Food and Culture) and “Healer of Herself” will be in Midnight and Indigo Magazine’s forthcoming speculative and horror fiction issue (October 2023). She received a BA in English from LSU (Geaux Tigers!). When she’s not spinning tales, Elnora spends her days breaking down prose and literature to teenagers, binge-watching entirely too much TV, and cooking with her husband. She lives in southern Louisiana but spent her early childhood in New England and Appalachia.

Phillip Gwynne

Go to Author Page

Phillip Gwynne is an Australian children’s book author. Before becoming a writer, he was a professional footballer, after which he worked as a teacher in Thailand and a programmer in Belgium. His books include the six-book Debt series: Catch the Zolt, Turn off the Lights, Bring Back Cerberus, Fetch the Treasure Hunter, Yamashita’s Gold, and Take a Life. He lives in Australia.

Margaret Hathaway & Karl Schatz

Go to Author Page

Margaret Hathaway is the author of numerous nonfiction titles, including The Year of the Goat: 40,000 Miles and the Quest for the Perfect Cheese, Living with Goats: Everything You Need to Know to Raise Your Own Backyard Herd, Food Lovers’ Guide to Maine: Best Local Specialties, Markets, Recipes, Restaurants & Events (Food Lovers Series), Portland, Maine Chef’s Table: Extraordinary Recipes From Casco Bay, and The New Portland, Maine, Chef’s Table: Extraordinary Recipes from the Coast of Maine. Her husband, Karl Schatz, is a photographer and creative director of Slow Food Portland. They live with their three children on a farm in Southern Maine, where they raise dairy goats, tend to a large garden and a small orchard, make cheese, and teach workshops on homesteading.

Sarah L. Hawthorn

Go to Author Page

Sarah L. Hawthorn is a Canadian horror and speculative fiction writer. Her debut novel, A Fate Worse Than Drowning, will be published by Sourcebooks in July 2026. She works in aging and health research and holds a bachelor’s in public relations from Mount Saint Vincent University. Their short fiction has been featured in The Arcanist and the Queer Blades anthology by From the Farther Trees Press. She loves to write and read anything spooky, especially gothic and eco-horror books. When she’s not writing, she enjoys watercolour painting, video games, and swimming. Sarah recently moved back to her hometown of Halifax, Nova Scotia, a maritime locale full of spooky stories she’s been devouring since elementary school.

Fran Hawthorne

Go to Author Page

Fran Hawthorne is an award-winning author and journalist who has covered health care, politics, finance, and business news for over twenty-five years. She was previously an editor and writer at Fortune, Business Week, and the Bergen Record, and has been a regular contributor to The New York Times and Newsday. She writes book reviews for The National and the New York Journal of Books. She is the author of eight books of nonfiction, including Ethical Chic: The Inside Story of the Companies We Think We Love; Pension Dumping: The Reasons, the Wreckage, the Stakes for Wall Street, and Inside the FDA: The Business and Politics Behind the Drugs We Take and the Food We Eat. Her debut novel, The Heirs, was published in 2018.

Christopher Healy

Go to Author Page

Christopher Healy is the author of the Hero’s Guide to Saving Your Kingdom and its sequels, as well as the Perilous Journey of Danger and Mayhem series. He also teaches writing and language arts in New Jersey, where he lives with his wife, two children, and a dog named Winston Bean.

Willow Heath

Go to Author Page

Willow Heath is a visual artist, illustrator, storyteller, creative crafter, and art instructor. Her debut picture book, Lasting Love (written by Caroline Wright) was published in 2019. In addition to her own illustrating and writing, she teaches illustration and painting in schools. Willow lives in Seattle with her husband and son.

Devorah Heitner

Go to Author Page

Devorah Heitner is an author, speaker, and an expert on young people’s relationship with digital media and technology. Her latest book on the subject, Growing Up in Public (TarcherPerigee, 2023) was an instant USA Today bestseller. She is also the author of Screenwise: Helping Kids Thrive (and Survive) in Their Digital World (Routledge, 2016); the second edition of the book was released in 2023. Dr. Heitner’s work has appeared in The New York Times, The Washington Post, The Wall Street Journal,  TIME magazine and Education Week. She has a Ph.D. in Media/Technology & Society from Northwestern University and has taught at DePaul and Northwestern.

Marc Herman

Go to Author Page

Marc Herman is the author of Searching for El Dorado, a description of his travels with Amazon gold miners. A longtime foreign correspondent, he is also the author of The Shores of Tripoli, a story about the Libyan revolt based on his reporting for The Atlantic, and The Wizard and the Volcano, a dispatch from a natural disaster in Indonesia. His current projects are One in a Million, a reconstruction of a high-stakes immigration case, and The Barcelona Apartment, which summarizes blunders he made while rebuilding an historic home in Barcelona, where he currently lives with his son.

Josuee Hernandez

Go to Author Page

Josuee Hernandez is a writer based in Portland, Oregon. His debut YA novel, What We Did to Each Other, will be published by Flux in September 2025. He holds a Bachelor of Arts in English Literature from the University of Oregon, a Master of Arts in English Literature from Portland State University, and a Master of Arts in Education, also from Portland State University. When he’s not writing, he serves as an educator for K-12 youth. Find him at www.josueehernandez.com.

Marc Hetherington

Go to Author Page

Marc Hetherington is the Raymond Dawson Professor of Political Science at the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill and the author of three books: Prius or Pickup?: How the Answers to Four Simple Questions Explain America’s Great Divide and Authoritarianism and Polarization in American Politics, co-authored with Jonathan Weiler, and Why Washington Won’t Work: Polarization, Political Trust, and the Governing Crisis, co-authored with Thomas J. Rudolph, which won the Alexander George Award from the International Society of Political Psychology. He and his work have been widely cited in mainstream media, including The New York Times, the Washington Post, and The Guardian. He lives in Chapel Hill, North Carolina.

Darla Himeles

Go to Author Page

Darla Himeles (they/them) is a Philadelphia-based poet and writer for young people, a mental health advocate, and an assistant teaching professor at Widener University. They are the author of the poetry collections Flesh Enough and Cleave (Get Fresh Books). A recipient of the Highlights Foundation’s Whole Novel Scholarship for Jewish Writers, Darla’s published poems and essays have been nominated for the Pushcart Prize and Best of the Net. They hold an AB in English from Bryn Mawr College, an MFA in poetry and poetry in translation from Drew University, and a PhD in American literature from Temple University. 

Lauren Ho

Go to Author Page

Lauren Ho is a reformed legal counsel who writes ownvoices stories. She is the author of Last Tang Standing and Lucie Yi Is Not a Romantic, both from Putnam. Bite Me, Royce Taslim, her debut YA novel, was sold to Disney-Hyperion at auction and was published in May 2024. Her forthcoming works include Two Lives With You, which will be released by Mindy’s Book Studio / Amazon in June 2026, and Sharpen Your Knives, will be published by Hachette / Sphere and Berkley in Winter 2027. Hailing from Malaysia, she lived in the United Kingdom, France and Luxembourg before moving with her family to Singapore, where she is ostensibly working on her next book.

Sara Jane Ho

Go to Author Page

Sara Jane Ho is a global etiquette leader for modern women in China and around the world. A Hong Kong native and graduate of Harvard Business School, Sara is the founder of Institute Sarita, China’s first etiquette school — named one of the World’s 50 Most Innovative Companies by Fast Company. She was named to Forbes‘ Women to Watch in Asia 2013 and BBC’s 100 Women 2015. She is also the author of the bestselling Chinese-language book Finishing Touch: Good Manners for the Debutante and the host of The Sara Show on Beijing Television, Tencent Video, and YouTube. She is the star of Netflix’s Mind Your Manners and the author of a forthcoming book of the same name, to be published by Hachette Go in April 2024.

Amanda Hollander

Go to Author Page

Amanda Hollander is a fiction writer and opera librettist based in Brooklyn. Her short story “A Feast of Butterflies” from the Mar/Apr issue of The Magazine of Fantasy & Science Fiction was included in Rich Horton’s Year’s Best Fantasy & Science Fiction, 2021. Another short story, “A Strange and Muensterous Desire” published by Diabolical Plots, made the 2022 Locus Recommended Reading List. She also has published fiction in Augur Magazine and Daily Science Fiction. A librettist fellow with the American Opera Project, she wrote the opera Quake, set by composer Kay Rhie, which will premiere through UCLA’s Department of Music in Spring 2023. In addition to her creative work, she holds a doctorate in Victorian and children’s literature from UCLA and has published several academic articles on fin-de-siècle children’s books.

Jennifer L. Holm

Go to Author Page

Jennifer L. Holm is the award-winning and bestselling author of multiple novels for young readers. Her debut novel, Our Only May Amelia, was a Newbery Honor book, and she has received two subsequent Newbery Honors for Penny from Heaven and Turtle in Paradise. Her novel Full of Beans, a sequel to Turtle in Paradise, won the Scott O’Dell Award for Historical Fiction. She is also the author of the Ginny Davis series; the Boston Jane series; the New York Times bestselling The Fourteenth Goldfish and its sequel, The Third Mushroom; The Lion of Mars (a New York Times bestseller) and a graphic novel adaptation of Turtle in Paradise, illustrated by Savanna Ganucheau (an IndieBound bestseller). Her latest middle grade novel, Outside, was published by Scholastic and was one of Amazon’s Best Books of 2025. With her brother Matthew, she is the co-creator of multiple award-winning and bestselling graphic novels, including the Babymouse series, the Squish series, and the Sunny series. She lives in California.

Jennifer L. Holm & Matthew Holm

Go to Author Page

Jennifer L. Holm and Matthew Holm are the brother-and-sister team behind over fifty best-selling and award-winning graphic novels and books for young readers, including the Eisner Award-winning Babymouse graphic novel series, which has over three million copies in print and has since branched out into picture books and middle-grade novels. They are the creators of the Squish series, the New York Times bestselling Sunny series, the Evil Princess vs. the Brave Knight series, the My First Comics board book series, and co-edited and contributed to the Comics Squad anthology series. New and forthcoming graphic novel publications include two volumes of The BIG Adventures of Babymouse and the seventh and eighth installments of the Sunny series.

Matthew Holm

Go to Author Page

Matthew Holm is an author, illustrator, graphic designer, and freelance writer. With his sister Jennifer, he is co-creator of multiple award-winning and New York Times bestselling graphic novels, including the Babymouse series, the Squish series, and the Sunny series. With Jonathan Follett, he is the co-author and illustrator of the middle grade novel Marvin and the Moths.

Jenny L. Howe

Go to Author Page

Jenny L. Howe is a professor of writing and literature at the University of Massachusetts, Dartmouth, where she teaches courses that focus on the representation of bodies and identities in children’s media. Her academic writing has been published in Young Adult Literature in the Composition Classroom: Essays on Practical Applications (MacFarland Press, 2018). She is the author of three romantic comedies for adults, published by St. Martin’s Press: The Make-Up Test (September 2022), On the Plus Side (December 2023), and How to Get a Life in Ten Dates (December 2024). She is the author of three YA novels, published by Delacorte: Love at Full Tilt, How (Not) to Renovate a Haunted House, and You’re a Mystery to Me. She lives in Massachusetts with her husband and the “excellent ears club” — i.e. their two rescue dogs, Tucker and Dale.

Simmone Howell

Go to Author Page

Simmone Howell is the author of four novels of young adult fiction, including Notes from the Teenage Underground, Everything Beautiful, Girl Defective, and Take Three Girls, co-written with Fiona Wood and Cath Crowley. She is a PhD candidate at LaTrobe University, where her research focuses on shared culture, dream-lives, life-writing, and the teenage experience. She has also taught young adult fiction and writing at the Royal Melbourne Institute of Technology and La Trobe University, and frequently facilitates creative writing workshops for schools and community groups. She lives in Melbourne.

Tonya Hurley

Go to Author Page

Tonya Hurley is an author, screenwriter, director, and producer, and the author of the Blessed series for teens, and the young adult novel, ghostgirl, which was an instant New York Times bestseller and has spawned two internationally bestselling sequels, been published in 22 foreign languages around the world, and optioned for film by Matthew Vaughan. Her independent films have been selected for film festivals around the world and broadcast on ABC, PBS, and IFC. Prior to her writing career, she was a music publicist for artists including Prince, George Michael, Morrissey, The Cure, and Depeche Mode. She lives with her family in Brooklyn.

Bryan Inman

Go to Author Page

Bryan Inman, a.k.a. the Rum Champion, is an rum expert, educator, and advocate who transformed his passion for the spirit into a full-time career. In addition to being the founder of the Rum Curious Club, the second largest rum club in the US, he hosts education events nationwide and serves as the National Brand Ambassador for both Rhum Barbancourt and El Dorado. He has tasted over 1,800 rums from 90 countries and has a collection of over 810 bottles. You can find him bartending at The Lucky Tiki in West Hollywood or talking rum via his ever-growing social media presence (@therumchampion).

Julie Iromuanya

Go to Author Page

Julie Iromuanya is the author of Mr. and Mrs. Doctor (Coffee House Press, 2015), a finalist for the PEN/Faulkner Award, the PEN/Robert W. Bingham Prize for Debut Fiction, the Etisalat Prize for Literature, and the National Book Critics Circle John Leonard Prize for Debut Fiction. Her latest novel, A Season of Light, was published by Algonquin Books and was a Book of the Month Pick for February 2025. Her scholarly-critical work most recently appears in Meridians: Feminism, Race, Transnationalism; Callaloo: A Journal of African American Arts and Letters; Afropolitan Literature as World Literature (Bloomsbury Publishing, 2020); and the Georgia Review. She is a 2020 George A. and Eliza Gardner Howard Foundation Fellow, and she was the inaugural Herbert W. Martin Fellow in Creative Writing at the University of Dayton. Iromuanya earned her PhD at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln. She is an assistant professor and director of undergraduate studies in the Program in Creative Writing at the University of Chicago and affiliate faculty of the Center for the Study of Gender and Sexuality and the Center for the Study of Race, Politics, and Culture. 

S. Jae-Jones

Go to Author Page

S. Jae-Jones is an artist and the New York Times best-selling author of Wintersong and Shadowsong, which have been published in ten foreign territories. Her most recent work is the Guardians of Dawn series, published by Wednesday Books. Zhara (2023) was selected by Barnes & Noble as its YA Book Club pick, and was followed by Ami (2024), Yuli (2025), and the forthcoming final installment Suhwa (2026). She was previously an editor at St. Martin’s Press in New York, and was also a contributor to the Pub(lishing) Crawl blog and co-host of their podcast. Born and raised in Los Angeles, she now lives in North Carolina.

Sarah Marie Jette

Go to Author Page

Sarah Marie Jette is a middle-grade author. Her novel, Our Fair Share will be published by Macmillan in May 2026. She was named Latinx in Publishing’s 2022-2023 Work in Progress Fellow and is the author of What the Wind Can Tell You. Sarah Marie spends her days with the most amazing people ever: her third-grade students! In 2022, Sarah Marie was a semifinalist for the Massachusetts Teacher of the Year. When she’s not teaching or writing, Sarah Marie crafts with her three children and snuggles with her cats.

Catherine Jinks

Go to Author Page

Catherine Jinks is an author of over a dozen books of children’s fiction and winner of numerous awards, including the Children’s Book Council of Australia “Book of the Year Award,” the Victorian Premier’s Literary Award, and the Aurealis Award. Her books include the acclaimed Evil Genius trilogy, the Bogler trilogy, Babylonne, The Reformed Vampire Support Group, Living Hell, The Abused Werewolf Rescue Group, The Paradise Trap, and Saving Thanehaven. She lives in New South Wales with her family.

Alaya Dawn Johnson

Go to Author Page

Alaya Dawn Johnson is an award-winning author of fiction for adults and young readers. Her debut literary adult novel, Trouble the Saints, won the World Fantasy Award for Best Novel. Her latest young adult novel, The Library of Broken Worlds, was published by Scholastic and Harper UK in 2023 and won the British Science Fiction Association Award for Young Readers. Her previous titles include The Summer Prince, which was longlisted for the National Book Award for Young People’s Literature, and Love is the Drug, which won the Andre Norton Award for YA Science Fiction and Fantasy. She has also contributed to numerous young adult anthologies, including Zombies vs Unicorns, Three Sides of a Heart, Feral Youth, and Come On In. Her novelette, “A Guide to the Fruits of Hawai’i” won the Nebula Award and appeared in The Best American Science Fiction and Fantasy, guest edited by Joe Hill. Her first collection of short fiction, Reconstruction, was published by Small Beer Press. She is one of five authors who collaborated with singer Janelle Monáe on her New York Times bestselling collection of short fiction, The Memory Librarian. She lives in Mexico, where she attained her Masters in Mesoamerican Studies.

Briana Johnson

Go to Author Page

Briana Johnson is a young adult writer and the author of If I Could Go Back and American Dream, both published by Peachtree Teen. She has a degree in Computer Network and Cybersecurity and was a mentee in the 2019 Pitch Wars class. She currently lives in Chicago with her family.

Kathleen B. Jones

Go to Author Page

Kathleen B. Jones was born and educated in New York, receiving a B.A. in Political Science from Brooklyn College, and a Ph.D. from the political science program at CUNY’s Graduate School and Center. After teaching women’s studies for two decades at San Diego State University, she resigned to focus on writing, recently earning an M.F.A. in fiction from Fairfield University. Her debut novel, Cities of Women was published by Keylight Books in 2023. Her scholarly writing includes six books published with academic presses—three monographs and three edited anthologies of critical essays. Diving for Pearls: A Thinking Journey with Hannah Arendt won the 2015 Barbara “Penny” Kanner Book Award from the Western Association of Women Historians. Kathleen’s essays and short fiction have appeared in Fiction International, Mr. Beller’s Neighborhood, The Briar Cliff Review, and The Los Angeles Review of Books. 

Melanie Katzman

Go to Author Page

Melanie Katzman is an author, psychologist, consultant, and sought-after expert in executive development, group dynamics, and leadership diversity. Connect First, her debut business book, was an instant national bestseller and a #1 Wall Street Journal bestseller. She founded Katzman Consulting in 1999 and has worked with businesses in 31 countries. She is also a partner of the global social enterprise Leaders’ Quest, serving a community of 12,000 members. She has held faculty positions in psychiatry at Weill-Cornell Medical Center and the University of London, was a senior fellow at Wharton Business School’s Center for Leadership and Change Management, and has been a visiting professor at numerous universities across the globe. She is co-creator and co-host of Women @ Work on SiriusXM, and has been featured in the Financial Times, The New York Times, Vanity Fair, and on ABC, CBS, and Lifetime. She lives in New York City.

Amy S. Kaufman

Go to Author Page

Amy S. Kaufman is the author of The Traitor of Sherwood Forest, a Robin Hood retelling forthcoming from Penguin Books in 2025. She is a medieval scholar who has written about the Middle Ages for both academic journals and popular venues, including The Washington Post, and is co-author of The Devil’s Historians: How Modern Extremists Abuse the Medieval Past (University of Toronto Press, 2020). Amy grew up in South Florida, but now lives in Vancouver, Canada, where she can’t stop taking pictures of the mountains. The Traitor of Sherwood Forest is her debut novel.

Lee Kelly

Go to Author Page

Lee Kelly is the author of With Regrets, published by Crooked Lane Books. She is also the author of two novels for young adults, City of Savages and A Criminal Magic. With Jennifer Thorne, she is the author The Antiquity Affair and The Starlets, published by Harper Muse. Before becoming a writer, she was an entertainment lawyer who practiced law in Los Angeles and New York. She lives with her husband and children in Pennsylvania.

Lee Kelly & Jennifer Thorne

Go to Author Page

Lee Kelly and Jennifer Thorne are the authors behind The Antiquity Affair, The Starlets, and My Fair Frauds, published by Harper Muse, and The Midnight Show, published by Crown. Lee Kelly is the author With Regrets and two novels for young adults, City of Savages and A Criminal Magic. Jennifer Thorne is the author of Lute, Diavola (a USA Today bestseller), published by Tor/Nightfire, and the picture book Construction Zoo. As Jenn Marie Thorne, she is the author of the YA novels The Wrong Side of Right, The Inside of Out, and Night Music.

Mikki Kendall

Go to Author Page

Mikki Kendall is a writer, speaker, and diversity consultant whose work tackles race, intersectionality, policing, gender, and many other topics. Her New York Times and IndieBound bestselling essay collection Hood Feminism: Notes From the Women That a Movement Forgot was published in February 2020 by Viking Books, and was named one of TIME‘s Must-Read Books of 2020, and has sold in six foreign territories. Her writing can be found in various outlets including Time.com, The Guardian, The Washington Post, Ebony, Essence, Salon, xoJane, Bustle, and Islamic Monthly, and was anthologized in Wonder Women of History from DC Comics. She has been interviewed by a number of media outlets including the BBC, NPR, Al Jazeera, MSNBC, Showtime, and appeared on The Daily Show with Trevor Noah and Good Morning America. Known as @Karnythia on Twitter, she has over 150,000 followers and created a number of viral hashtags, including #solidarityisforwhitewomen, #HistoricPOC, and #fasttailedgirls. She is also the author of the graphic novel Amazons, Abolitionists, and Activists: A Graphic History of Women’s Fight for Their Rights. She lives with her family in Chicago.

Sakika Kikuchi

Go to Author Page Go to Portfolio

Sakika Kikuchi is an illustrator and graphic designer. She completed her MA in children’s book illustration at the Cambridge School of Art in the UK, and lives outside of Tokyo in her native Japan. She is the illustrator of Show Us Where You Live, Humpback, written by Beryl Young and published by Greystone Kids in 2021. Her latest picture book Milo Walking, written by James Howe, was published by Abrams in November 2023.

Amy Kim

Go to Author Page

Amy Kim is the author of The Rema Chronicles, Volume 1: Realm of the Blue Mist, which was named an American Library Association Best Graphic Novel for Children. She also created Sorcerers & Secretaries, a graphic novel duology, and many short stories, including an adaptation of Deep Trouble for Goosebumps: Terror Trips by R.L. Stine. Amy has been drawing in earnest since age ten and strives to tell stories that touch the heart, inspire the imagination, and pay homage to her roots in South Korea, New York City, and her childhood home of Upstate New York.

Gayle Kirshenbaum

Go to Author Page

Gayle Kirshenbaum is a writer and community organizer whose work has appeared in Newsweek, The New York Daily News, The Huffington Post, Ms., George, The New York Times, and Tikkun. She’s written about motherhood, parenting and race, labor organizing, reproductive rights, and Jewish identity. She is a co-founder of Hand in Hand, a national organization that supports employers of domestic workers to be fair employers. As an editor at Ms., she was a co-recipient of the Exceptional Merit Media Award from the National Women’s Political Caucus. She attended the University of Michigan, where she was a recipient of the Hopwood Award for essay writing. She lives in Brooklyn, New York, where she is working on a novel about women’s friendships and the construction of racial and political identity set in the college town of Ann Arbor, Michigan, shortly after the lead poisoning of the water supply in nearby Flint.

Ellen Klages

Go to Author Page

Ellen Klages is a critically-acclaimed and award-winning author of fiction for children and adults. Her first novel for young readers, The Green Glass Sea, won several awards, including the Scott O’Dell Award for Historical Fiction, and was followed by White Sands, Red Menace and Out of Left Field, winner of the New York Historical Society Children’s History Book Prize. She is also an acclaimed author of short fiction: most recently, her novella Passing Strange won the World and British Fantasy Awards for Best Novella, and her story “Amicae Aeternum” was adapted into an award-winning short film. Her work has been a finalist for the Hugo, Nebula, and Locus Awards and has been translated into eight languages. She lives in San Francisco.

Ambelin Kwaymullina

Go to Author Page

Ambelin Kwaymullina is an Aboriginal author and illustrator who belongs to the Palyku people of the Pilbara region of Western Australia. She works across a wide range of forms including poetry, art, and both fiction and non-fiction. Ambelin is a multi-award winning writer whose work has been published across the world including in Europe, the United States, China and South Korea. Her latest YA novel, Liar’s Test, was published by Knopf in May 2024.

Ambelin & Ezekiel Kwaymullina

Go to Author Page

Ambelin Kwaymullina and Ezekiel Kwaymullina are a brother-sister team of Aboriginal writers who come from the Palyku people of the Pilbara region of Western Australia.
