The Things She’s Seen, their first joint young adult novel (published as Catching Teller Crow in Australia), received rave reviews and won multiple awards, including the Victorian Premier’s Literary Prize for Young Adult Fiction and the Aurealis Award for Best Young Adult Novel. They have previously collaborated on a number of short novels and picture books. Ambelin is also the author of The Tribe series of novels, including The Foretelling of Georgie Spider, The Disappearance of Ember Crow, and The Interrogation of Ashala Wolf.

Margo Lanagan

Go to Author Page

Margo Lanagan is an acclaimed and award-winning author of fiction for young readers. She is a two-time winner of the Michael L. Printz Honor, for Black Juice, a collection of short fiction, and her novel Tender Morsels. Her other short fiction collections include Red Spikes, White Time, Yellowcake, Phantom Limbs, and Stray Bats. She has won and received dozens of nominations for prestigious awards including the Aurealis, the World Fantasy Award, and a number of Australian Premier Literary Awards. With Scott Westerfeld and Deborah Biancotti, she is the co-author of the New York Times bestselling Zeroes trilogy. Her latest novel is Spindle Sisters, forthcoming from Knopf and Allen & Unwin. She lives in Sydney.

Justine Larbalestier

Go to Author Page

Justine Larbalestier is the award-winning author of eight critically acclaimed novels for young adults. Her most recent novel, My Sister Rosa, was an Edgar Award Finalist for Best Young Adult Novel. Her other novels include the Magic or Madness trilogy (winner of the Andre Norton Award), How to Ditch Your Fairy, Liar, and Razorhurst. With Holly Black, she edited the anthology Zombies vs. Unicorns and has contributed essays and short fiction to a number of collections. She was previously an academic and edited a scholarly collection of feminist science fiction and published The Battle of The Sexes in Science Fiction. She and her husband, author Scott Westerfeld, will publish their jointly-authored adult debut, The Mortons, in 2026.

Justine Larbalestier & Scott Westerfeld

Go to Author Page

Justine Larbalestier & Scott Westerfeld are the authors of The Mortons, their jointly-authored adult debut. The literary suspense novel, slated for simultaneous publication in 2026, sold at auction in the US, in a pre-empt in the UK, and in a six-house auction in Australia. Justine is an award-winning author of several novels for young adults, including My Sister Rosa, How to Ditch Your Fairy, Liar, Razorhurst, and Zombies v Unicorns (with Holly Black). Scott is best known as the #1 New York Times bestselling author of over 20 books for adults and young readers, including the Uglies and Impostors series, and the Leviathan trilogy.

Kirby Larson

Go to Author Page

Kirby Larson is the award-winning and bestselling author of numerous books for young readers. Her debut novel Hattie Big Sky won the Newbery Honor and was a New York Times bestseller. With Mary Nethery, she is the author of the New York Times bestselling picture book Nubs: The True Story of a Mutt, a Marine & a Miracle (with Major Brian Dennis), Two Bobbies: A True Story of Hurricane Katrina, Friendship, and Survival, and the forthcoming 23 Dogs and 1 Cat (Bloomsbury, 2026). She written the acclaimed Dogs of World War II series — Duke, Dash (winner of the Scott O’Dell Award for Historical Fiction), Liberty, and Code Word Courage; the Audacity Jones series, which received an Edgar nomination; the Shermy and Shake series of chapter books, illustrated by Shinji Fujioka; and Gut Reaction, a contemporary middle grade novel co-written with her daughter, Quinn Wyatt. She also has written picture books and novels for American Girl and the Dear America series. She lives in Kenmore, Washington with her husband.

Oren Lavie

Go to Author Page

Oren Lavie is a musician, director, playwright, and author of children’s books. He has released a number of critically acclaimed albums, including The Opposite Side of the Sea. The music video for “Her Morning Elegance” was nominated for a Grammy, and received over 30 million views on YouTube and his song “A Dance ‘round The Memory Tree” was featured on the soundtrack for Disney’s The Chronicles of Narnia: Prince Caspian. He is the author of two picture books: The Bear Who Wasn’t There: And the Fabulous Forest (Akashic) and Truman Toad and the Quest for the Perfect Hug (Enchanted Lion), which have been published in a number of foreign languages. He lives in Tel Aviv, Israel.

Speed Levitch

Go to Author Page

Timothy “Speed” Levitch is an actor, tour guide, speaker, author, and voice actor, based in Kansas City and New York. A licensed New York City tour guide since 1992, he has worked with Apple and Gray Line Tours as a tour bus guide, and quickly attracted a cult following for his distinctive, fast-talking tours — The New York Times called him “the Walt Whitman of the double decker bus.” He was later the subject of The Cruise, an award-winning documentary from Academy Award-nominated director Bennett Miller, which was screened at a number of international film festivals. He has frequently collaborated with Academy Award-nominated director Richard Linklater, who directed him in Up to Speed, a comedic docu-series currently available to stream on Hulu, the cult animated film Waking Life, and the smash hit comedy School of Rock. Recently, he appeared as himself on HBO’s High Maintenance. He has performed his monologue “Midtown Loop” at sold-out venues in New York City, and continues to give public and private tours.

Johnny Marciano

Go to Author Page

Johnny Marciano is a New York Times bestselling author and illustrator. He is the co-author of the Klawde series of illustrated chapter books with Emily Chenoweth. With Sophie Blackall, he is the co-creator of the Witches of Benevento series. He has published several standalone middle-grade novels, including The Nine Lives of Alexander Baddenfield and The No-Good Nine, and three books of nonfiction for adults: Anonyponymous: The Forgotten People Behind Everyday Words, Toponymity: An Atlas of Words, and Whatever Happened to the Metric System?: How America Kept Its Feet. His picture book How Did Humans Go Extinct?, illustrated by Paul Hoppe, was published by Black Sheep/Akashic. His latest work is the middle grade series The 66th Rebirth of Frankie Caridi — the first and second books are releasing in 2024 and 2025, respectively. As John Bemelmans Marciano, he has authored and illustrated five Madeline books, continuing the series created by his grandfather, Ludwig Bemelmans, and is writing the first graphic novel starring Madeline, to be illustrated by LeUyen Pham and Alex Puvilland, for publication in 2027. He lives in New Jersey with his wife and daughter.

Britney S. Lewis

Go to Author Page

Britney S. Lewis is a #1 New York Times bestselling and award-winning YA author. Her novel, Blood Moon (Page Street, 2025) was an instant #1 New York Times bestseller, an instant Indie bestseller, a USA Today bestseller, an Indie Next Pick and was selected for FaeCrate and OwlCrate book boxes. The Blood Chronicles will continue with two additional novels, Blood Lust (Page Street, 2027) and and as-yet untitled third book. Her other YA novels include The Undead Truth of Us (Hyperion, 2022) and The Dark Place (Hyperion, 2023). She has a B.A. in corporate communications with an emphasis in business and art. She strongly supports We Need Diverse Books, and she’s an avid follower of #DVpit and #BVM. When Britney isn’t daydreaming about new stories, she can be found binge-watching TV shows with her husband and her pup or practicing West Coast Swing. She lives in Kansas City.

Rosina Lippi

Go to Author Page

Rosina Lippi is a former academic and the author of over a dozen books of fiction and scholarly work. Her critically acclaimed debut novel, Homestead, won the PEN/Hemingway Award and was shortlisted for the UK’s prestigious Orange Prize. She also wrote two later novels, Tied to the Tracks and The Pajama Girls of Lambert Square, as Rosina Lippi. As Sara Donati, she is the author of the Wilderness series of novels, which has over a million copies in print. The series comprises six novels: Into the Wilderness, Dawn on a Distant Shore, Lake in the Clouds, Fire Along the Sky, Queen of Swords, and The Endless Forest. She wrote two additional books, The Gilded Hour and Where the Light Enters, that are set in the same historical fiction universe. Her latest novel, The Sweet Blue Distance, is a historical epic set in the American West and was released in April 2024. She lives on Puget Sound with her family.

Bess Lovejoy

Go to Author Page

Bess Lovejoy is a freelance writer whose work has appeared in The New York Times, The Wall Street Journal, Time, The Believer, Lapham’s Quarterly, The Boston Globe, The Public Domain Review, Atlas Obscura, and many other outlets. She is the author of Rest in Pieces: The Curious Fates of Famous Corpses, which was named an Amazon Best Book of 2013 and has been translated into three foreign languages. She was an editor for Schott’s Almanac, the Smithsonian website, and Mental Floss. She is based in Seattle.

Leil Lowndes

Go to Author Page

Leil Lowndes is an author and internationally recognized communications expert who specializes in subconscious interactions. She has conducted hundreds of seminars in the US and around the world for major corporations, associations, and the general public, and frequently appears as a guest expert on national television shows and major news networks. She has authored ten bestselling books on communications — most recently, How to Talk to Anyone at Work: 72 Little Tricks for Big Success Communicating on the Job — and is published in over 26 foreign languages. She lives in New York City.

Melina Marchetta

Go to Author Page

Melina Marchetta is a critically-acclaimed and award-winning author of books for adults and young readers. Her young adult novel, On the Jellicoe Road, won the Michael L. Printz Medal, and she has been nominated for dozens of Australian literary prizes, including the Prime Minister’s Literary Award, and was the recipient of the NSW Premier’s Special Award in 2021. She adapted her debut novel, Looking for Alibrandi, into a feature film, and won the Australian Film Institute Award and an Independent Film Award for Best Screenplay. Her other novels for young readers include Saving Francesca, The Piper’s Son (longlisted for the Miles Franklin Award), and The Lumatere Chronicles trilogy. Her novels for adults include The Place on Dalhousie and the literary crime novel Tell the Truth, Shame the Devil. Her most recent works are a series of seven children’s chapter books and a picture book about a mischievous young girl named Zola, published by Penguin Australia. Her work has been translated into 18 foreign languages and published in 20 countries. She lives in Sydney.

Zara Marielle

Go to Author Page

Zara Marielle is the product of a Northern Californian hippy town and a rustic village high in the French Alps. A former ESL teacher, she loves to travel and has also lived in Canada, Venezuela, England, and Scotland. Zara earned a master’s in creative writing from the University of Edinburgh in 2015. Her debut novel, The Café of Infinite Doors, will be published by Union Square & Co in April 2026. When she isn’t working on contemporary fantasy novels, she spends her time inventing much sillier, spontaneous content in improv shows and workshops.

John Marsden

Go to Author Page

John Marsden was the author of more than 40 books for young readers, including Tomorrow When the War Began, the first in a series with seven books and three tie-in novels, which was adapted into a feature film in 2010. His novels have sold over five million copies worldwide and he was honored with every major award in Australia for children’s fiction. His passion for education led him to found two schools in Australia, Candleback and Alice Miller, which place emphasis on the creative arts, enroll almost 400 students and inspired The Art of Growing Up, a nonfiction book about education and parenting.

John passed away in December 2024 at the age of 74.

Rachel M. Marsh

Go to Author Page

Rachel M. Marsh earned her MFA in Creative Writing for Children from Simmons University in Boston. She is the author of Rougarou Magic and Grandpere’s Ghost Swamp, published by Greenwillow. She now lives in New Orleans where she and her husband eat the world’s best food and renovate their almost-hundred-year-old house.

Sarah Glenn Marsh

Go to Author Page

Sarah Glenn Marsh is an author of books for readers of all ages. Her debut adult novel, Our Rogue Fates, will be published by Alcove Press in 2026. Her YA titles include Fear the Drowning Deep, Dread the Harvest Moon, the Reign of the Fallen series, and The Girls Are Never Gone. Her picture book titles include Selfie Sebastian, Ninita’s Big World: The True Story of a Deaf Pygmy Marmoset, The Bug Girl: Maria Merian’s Scientific Vision, Ellie’s Crayon Adventure and The Mystery of the Love List. She has also authored the middle grade title Your Birthstone Book, published by Running Press Kids. Her new and forthcoming books include the picture books Bigfoot’s Big Heart and Ghost Army, and several books in the “How to Spot” series from Running Press Kids, including How to Spot a Fairy, How to Spot a Magical Woodland Creature, How to Spot a Mermaid, and The Monster Manual. She lives with her family in Richmond, Virginia.

John Marshall

Go to Author Page

John Marshall is a nine-time Emmy Award-winning writer, producer, and director, and wrote and hosted numerous weekly TV shows in Maine for over a decade. He created the “Sunset Selfies” photo series, which received significant media attention and was picked up by The Guardian. He is the author of the memoir Wide-Open World: How Volunteering Around the Globe Changed One Family’s Lives Forever.

Kate Maupin

Go to Author Page

Kate Maupin is an award-winning author, educator, foster child advocate, and reformed Super Mom. She is a genre-hopping author, with work originating in academic nonfiction. Her debut novel, Dead Batteries, sold at auction and will be published by Crown in 2026. She is a national speaker on education and parenting topics, and is a recent retiree of the executive board of the Connecticut Association for the Gifted. Her work with populations of high needs children, as well as her own journey in special needs parenting, brought her to the realization that all parents can benefit from an antibody to “super parenting”. Kate currently lives in the wilds of Connecticut with her husband, two sons, and a truly outrageous number of pets. 

Patricia McCormick

Go to Author Page

Patricia McCormick is a two-time National Book Award finalist whose debut memoir, If It’s True, I’d Want to Know will be published by St. Martin’s in 2027. She is the author of several critically acclaimed novels and picture books, including Cut, Never Fall Down, The Plot to Kill Hitler, Sgt. Reckless, the True Story of the Little Horse Who Became a Hero and Sold, which was adapted into a feature film in 2016. She is also the co-author of I am Malala. According to the American Library Association, Sold was the number one most banned book in the U.S. in 2025. Her latest picture books are The Bicycle, a nonfiction picture book coauthored with Mevan Babakar, and Sam’s Neighborhood, the story of how a little deaf girl taught her neighbors to sign. Her books have been named to the New York Times Notable Books list, Publishers Weekly Best Books list, NPR’s Best Books list and iTunes Best Books lists. She has received the New York Foundation on the Arts fellowship, the Virginia Center for the Creative Arts fellowship, and a MacDowell Center fellowship. She attended Rosemont College, the Columbia University Graduate School of Journalism and has an MFA from the New School. She lives in Sag Harbor, New York.

Kara McDowell

Go to Author Page

Kara McDowell is the author of five novels for young adults, including One Way or Another and This Might Get Awkward, published by Scholastic; and The Prince & the Apocalypse and Heir, Apparently, published by Wednesday Books. Her debut adult romance, The Write Off, was published by Berkley in April 2026. She received her BA in English Literature and worked as a freelance writer and blogger before publishing her debut novel, Just for Clicks. She lives with her husband and three sons in Arizona.

Chris McGowan

Go to Author Page

Chris McGowan is an author, educator, and paleontologist who received his PhD from London University. He is the former curator of vertebrate paleontology at the Royal Ontario Museum in Toronto and professor of zoology at the University of Toronto and has written over fifteen books of nonfiction for children and adults, including Dinosaur Discovery: Everything You Need to Be a Paleontologist and The Dragon Seekers: How An Extraordinary Circle Of Fossilists Discovered The Dinosaurs And Paved The Way For Darwin. Born in England, he has lived and worked in Canada for over fifty years.

Lauren McLaughlin

Go to Author Page

Lauren McLaughlin is a screenwriter and the author of eight books for children and young adults. She began her film career producing music videos and commercials, and went on to produce several feature films for Lionsgate. Her produced screenplays include Prisoners of Love, Specimen, and Hypercube. She is the author of five novels for young adults: Cycler, (Re)cycler, Scored, The Free, and Send Pics, and has written three picture books: Wonderful You and two books in the Mitzi Tulane: Preschool Detective series. Her debut collection of short fiction, The Perfect Man and Others, was released in 2021. She lives with her family in London.

Felicity McLean

Go to Author Page

Felicity McLean is a ghostwriter, journalist, and author. She has ghostwritten six books, including the award-winning Body Lengths with Olympian Leisel Jones, and has also authored a picture book. As a journalist, she has conducted interviews with dozens of authors, including James Patterson, Lee Child, and Nicholas Sparks. Her debut novel, The Van Apfel Girls Are Gone was released to critical acclaim and has been published in six countries. She lives in Australia.

Mary McMyne

Go to Author Page

Mary McMyne is the author of The Book of Gothel (2022) and A Rose by Any Other Name (2024), published by Redhook and Orbit UK. She has published short stories, poems, and essays in venues such as Gulf Coast, Redivider, Strange Horizons, Apex Magazine, Painted Bride Quarterly, and Southern Humanities Review. She is the recipient of the Faulkner-Wisdom Prize for a Novel-in-Progress, a grant from the Sustainable Arts Foundation, and a National Endowment for the Arts Parent Fellowship to Vermont Studio Center, among other honors. Her debut poetry chapbook, Wolf Skin (Dancing Girl Press, 2014), won the Elgin Chapbook Award. A graduate of the New York University MFA Program, she is an Associate Professor of Creative Writing at Lake Superior State University. 

Aline Mello

Go to Author Page

Aline Mello is a writer, editor, and Undocupoet fellow. Her debut poetry collection, More Salt Than Diamond, was published by Andrews McMeel. Her work has been published in Catapult, The Georgia Review, The New Republic, Wildness Journal, and Saint Katherine Review, among others. In addition to writing, Aline teaches cross-stitching and embroidery to adults and tries to make her own clothes and earrings at home. She is originally from Brazil and now lives in Atlanta, Georgia with her dog and books.

Chris Mercogliano

Go to Author Page

Chris Mercogliano is the author of Making It Up As We Go Along, the Story of the Albany Free School (Heinemann, 1998), Teaching the Restless, One School’s Remarkable No-Ritalin Approach to Helping Children Learn and Succeed (Beacon Press, 2004), How to Grow a School: Starting and Sustaining Schools That Work (Oxford Village Press, 2006), In Defense of Childhood: Protecting Kids’ Inner Wildness (Beacon Press, 2007), and A School Must Have a Heart (Oxford Village Press, 2014). His essays, commentaries, and reviews have appeared in numerous newspapers, magazines, and journals, as well as in seven anthologies; and he has also been featured on National Public Radio’s “All Things Considered,” Canadian Broadcasting Corporation Radio’s “Ideas,” and many other nationally syndicated radio shows. The father of two wonderful daughters, he lives with his wife Betsy on a one-acre farm in downtown Albany, New York.

Marissa Meyer

Go to Author Page

Marissa Meyer is the #1 New York Times–bestselling author of The Lunar Chronicles series (soon to be adapted into an animated feature film by Warner Bros. Pictures Animation and Locksmith Animation, for release in theaters in November 2028), Heartless, the Renegades trilogy, the Fortuna Beach duology, and the Gilded duology. Her latest fairytale retelling, The House Saphir, was an instant New York Times and #1 Indie bestseller. She edited the anthology Serendipity: Ten Romantic Tropes, Transformed. Her debut middle grade novel, Let It Glow, co-written with Joanne Levy, was published in October 2024 and was a New York Times and Indie bestseller, and a sequel will be released in 2027. We Could be Magic, a new original graphic novel illustrated by Joelle Murray, was published in June 2025. The Escape Game, a YA mystery thriller co-written with Tamara Moss, was published by Putnam in 2026 and was a #1 New York Times bestseller and #1 Indie bestseller. Marissa created and hosted The Happy Writer podcast where she interviewed authors on how to overcome various obstacles and bring more joy to the writing process, and released a companion non-fiction/self-help book of the same name in 2025. Her books have sold over eight million copies worldwide and have been published in 37 foreign territories. She lives in Tacoma, Washington, with her husband and their two daughters.

Brenda Miller

Go to Author Page

Brenda Miller is the author of five essay collections, the most recent of which are A Braided Heart: Essays on Writing and Form, and ​The Next Draft​: Inspiring Craft Talks from the Rainier Writing Workshop published by the University of Michigan Press.. She has co-authored three editions of Tell It Slant: Creating, Refining and Publishing Creative Nonfiction with Suzanne Paola. She was the Editor-in-Chief of the Bellingham Review for 17 years and her own poetry has appeared in Tupelo Quarterly, Sweet, Bellevue Literary Review, and Psaltery and Lyre. She is the recipient of six Pushcart Prizes. She lives in Washington state, where she is a Professor of English at Western Washington University, and associate faculty at the Rainier Writing Workshop

Catherine Gilbert Murdock

Go to Author Page

Catherine Gilbert Murdock is a former academic and the author of eight novels for children and young adults. Her latest novel for young readers, Da Vinci’s Cat, was published in May 2021 by Greenwillow/HarperCollins. Her novel The Book of Boy was a Newbery Honor book and has sold into ten foreign territories. She is also the author of the Dairy Queen series, comprised of three young adult novels and one for middle grade readers, and two historical fantasy novels: Princess Ben and Wisdom’s Kiss. She lives in Philadelphia.

Kiyash Monsef

Go to Author Page

Kiyash Monsef is a writer, creative director, and Emmy-nominated producer who has worked in a number of media, including television, short films, comic books, and video games. His debut novel, Once There Was was an instant New York Times bestseller, was selected as Target’s Book of the Year for Young Readers, and was named a Finalist for the 2024 William C. Morris YA Debut Award. His next middle grade novel, Bird of a Thousand Stories, was published in February 2025. He is also a Webby-nominated designer of innovative conversational and voice interface experiences, and has worked for Google, Motorola, Nokia, and The New York Public Library, among many other clients. His short fiction has been featured in Byliner, New Millennium Writings, and Penumbra’s New Fiction. He lives in Berkeley, California.

Angela Montoya

Go to Author Page

Angela Montoya is the author of three young adult romantasy novels: Sinner’s Isle, A Cruel Thirst, and Carnival Fantástico, all published by Joy Revolution Books. She was named a Publishers Weekly Fall 2023 Flying Start, and has participated as a Round 5 Author-Mentor Match mentee and was selected to be a mentee in Pitch Wars 2020. She lives in Northern California with her family.

Jaclyn Moriarty

Go to Author Page

Jaclyn Moriarty is the bestselling author of over a dozen novels, and has been translated into over ten foreign languages. She is known for her young adult and middle grade novels, including the Ashbury-Brookfield series, the prize-winning Colours of Madeleine series, and the Kingdom & Empires series. Her standalone novels include I Have a Bed Made of Buttermilk Pancakes and The Spell Book of Listen Taylor. Her debut adult novel, Gravity is the Thing, was published by HarperCollins to great acclaim. Her middle grade novels, The Stolen Prince of Cloudburst, The Astonishing Chronicles of Oscar from Elsewhere, The Secret of Lillian Velvet, and The Accidental World-Slip of Astrid Verity are published in the US by Levine Querido. Her next adult novel, Time Travel for Beginners, will be published by Berkley in August 2026, and simultaneously in the UK by HQ, and Australia by Ultimo Press. She has received a number of Premier’s Literary Awards, the Aurealis Award, and the Boston Globe-Horn Book Honor Award. She lives in Sydney.

Tamara Moss

Go to Author Page

Tamara Moss grew up in Perth, Western Australia. She has a BA majoring in Asian Studies and a teaching diploma, which she used to teach primary school for ten years. Inspired by her students’ love of adventure, Tamara wrote the Lintang series, beginning with Lintang and the Pirate Queen. Her latest novel, The Escape Game, is a YA murder mystery co-authored with New York Times-bestselling author Marissa Meyer, was an #1 New York Times and a #1 Indie bestseller. Her debut solo-authored YA novel, Five Voices, will be published by Allen & Unwin in fall 2026.

Riss Neilson

Go to Author Page

Riss M. Neilson is the USA Today bestseller of A Love Like the Sun. She’s a magna cum laude graduate of Rhode Island College, where she won the English department’s Jean Garrigue Award for creative writing. Her debut young adult novel, Deep in Providence, was a 2022 finalist for the New England Book Awards. She has two daughters, a cat, two dog and two bunnies named Michael Myers and Casper the Friendly Ghost. She was born and raised in Providence. When she’s not writing, she’s watching anime or playing video games with her kids.

Hiền Nguyễn

Go to Author Page

Hiền Nguyễn is a speculative fiction writer who hails from the Midwest. By day she is a social science researcher and by night she writes about Vietnamese ghosts, monsters, and mythology. Her debut novel Twin Tides, will be published by Delacorte in December 2025. She is interested in the uplifting and haunting forms of human connection, and how SFF writing can lay those bare. An indoor cat disguised as a human, Hiền enjoys going bird watching with her significant other, binging K-pop variety shows, and absorbing hot pot broth like a sponge.

Garth Nix

Go to Author Page

Garth Nix is the internationally bestselling author of over 30 books for readers of all ages, including the New York Times bestselling Old Kingdom series, the Keys to the Kingdom series, the Seventh Tower series, Frogkisser!, Angel Mage, The Left-Handed Booksellers of London, The Sinister Booksellers of Bath, and Sir Hereward and Mister Fitz: Stories of the Witch Knight and the Puppet Sorcerer, and We Do Not Welcome Our Ten-Year-Old Overlord. His next adult novel, the science fiction epic Massif, will be published simultaneously in the US, UK, and Australia in Fall 2026. His forthcoming titles include a third installment in the Booksellers series, The Even-Handed Booksellers of Edinburgh. With Sean Williams, he is the co-author of the Troubletwisters series, the Have Sword, Will Travel series, and Spirit Animals: Blood Ties. More than six million copies of his books have been sold around the world and his work has been translated into 40 languages. He has received countless awards and nominations in the US and Australia for his novels and short fiction. He lives in Sydney, Australia, with his wife and two children.

Sabina Nordqvist

Go to Author Page

Sabina Nordqvist began writing as a way to distract herself from chronic pain and illness, and before long, she couldn’t stop creating imaginary worlds and swoony book boyfriends. When not immersed in her latest project, she’s likely doing physical therapy, reading, or searching for answers to her latest mystery symptom. A polyglot with three nationalities, she’s spent many years abroad and loves nerding out over intercultural communication and the languages she’s picked up along the way. It’s All in Your Head is her debut novel.

Deborah Noyes

Go to Author Page

Deborah Noyes is an editor, photographer, and author of numerous books for children and adults. She has edited several well-received collections of short fiction, including The Restless Dead, Gothic!, and Sideshow, and is the author of four novels: Angel and Apostle and Captivity for adults, and The Ghosts of Kerfol and Plague in the Mirror for young adults. She also writes nonfiction for young readers, including Ten Days a Madwoman: The Daring Life and Turbulent Times of the Original “Girl” Reporter, Nellie Bly (shortlisted for the New York Historical Society Children’s History Prize), The Magician and the Spirits: Harry Houdini and the Curious Pastime of Communicating with the Dead, Tooth & Claw: The Dinosaur Wars, A Hopeful Heart: Louisa May Alcott Before Little Women, and Lady Icarus: Balloonomania and the Brief, Bold Life of Sophie Blanchard. She is also the author the short fiction collection We Are All His Creatures: Tales of P.T. Barnum, The Greatest Showman. Her recent and forthcoming books include An Outbreak of Witchcraft, a graphic novel illustrated by M. Duffy and We Are the Trees, a nonfiction picture book about conservationist Suzanne Simard, illustrated by Jessica Lanan, which will be published by Neal Porter Books. She lives in Maine.

Stefanie O’Connell

Go to Author Page

Stefanie O’Connell is a writer covering ambition, money and power in her award-winning newsletter “Too Ambitious”, with bylines in Bloomberg, CNBC, Glamour UK, Newsweek, USA Today and Business Insider. Her latest book, The Ambition Penalty, was published by Basic Venture in 2026. She is also produced and hosted Real Simple magazine’s ‘Money Confidential’ podcast (a 2023 Webby Award Winner, a 2022 Eddie & Ozzie award-winner, a 2022 Signal Award winner, and one of Spotify’s “Best New Shows of 2021”). A go-to millennial expert, Stefanie has appeared on ABC World News, Yahoo Finance, CBSN, Fox & Friends, Fox Business, Bloomberg, The Doctors and local news stations around the US. A graduate of New York University, Stefanie lives in New York City with her husband and daughter.

Johan Olander

Go to Author Page

Johan Olander is an author and illustrator of numerous books for young readers. He wrote and illustrated A Field Guide to Monsters, A Field Guide to Aliens, and My Robots. He is also the illustrator of The Need to Feed: Recipes for Developing a Healthy Obsession for Deeply Satisfying Foods by underground music legend Lydia Lunch; Parade?, a limited edition children’s book by French artist Philippe Parreno; and An Eyeball in My Garden, a collection of humorous poetry selected and edited by Laura Wynkoop and Jennifer Cole Judd. He lives in Brooklyn with his family.

Alyssa Palombo

Go to Author Page

Alyssa Palombo is an author of historical fiction. Her novels include The Violinist of Venice, The Most Beautiful Woman in Florence, The Spellbook of Katrina Van Tassel, and The Borgia Confessions. Her latest book, The Assassin of Venice, was published by Crooked Lane in June 2024. An avid reader, she is also a classically trained musician with a love of heavy metal and rock music. She graduated summa cum laude from Canisius College in 2011. When not traveling as much as she can, she lives and writes in Buffalo, NY.

Susanne Paola Antonetta

Go to Author Page

Susanne Paola Antonetta is an award-winning poet, memoirist, and author of nonfiction. She is the author of Body Toxic: An Environmental Memoir, A Mind Apart: Travels in a Neurodiverse World, and Make Me a Mother: A Memoir. Her latest project, The Devil’s Castle: Nazi Eugenics, Euthanasia, and How Psychiatry’s Troubled History Reverberates Today, was published by Counterpoint in September 2025, and received a starred review from Publishers Weekly. With Brenda Miller, she is the author of Tell It Slant: Creating, Refining, and Publishing Creative Nonfiction. Among the awards her work has won are a New York Times Notable Book distinction, an American Book Award, a Library Journal Best Science Book of the Year Award, and a Pushcart Prize. Her essays and poetry have appeared in The New York Times, The Washington Post, Orion, and Seneca Review. She lives in Bellingham, Washington.

Sajni Patel

Go to Author Page

Sajni Patel is an author of fiction for readers of all ages. She is the author of the romance novels The Trouble With Hating You and First Love, Take Two, published by Forever, and Isha, Unscripted and The Design of Us, published by Berkley. Her contemporary YA novels include The Knockout, My Sister’s Big Fat Indian Wedding, and Sleepless in Dubai. A Drop of Venom, her debut YA fantasy novel, was published by Rick Riordan Presents and was followed by a sequel, A Touch of Blood. Her next YA novel, Cerulean, is forthcoming in Summer 2027 from Wednesday Books. Her short fiction appeared in the contemporary anthology Together Apart. Prior to her writing career, she worked in aerospace research with the chairman of the Indian Space Research Organisation.

George Pendle

Go to Author Page

George Pendle is a journalist and the author of three books: Strange Angel: The Otherworldly Life of Rocket Scientist John Whiteside Parsons, The Remarkable Millard Fillmore: The Unbelievable Life of a Forgotten President, and Death: A Life. Strange Angel was adapted into a television series on CBS All Access with Ridley Scott executive producing, and has aired its second season. His writing has appeared in The New York Times, The Sunday Times, the Financial Times, and he previously wrote for the New York City Department of Parks and Recreation.

Nadi Reed Perez

Go to Author Page

Nadi Reed Perez is an author and writer for The Call of the Flame, a fantasy fiction podcast. They live near Denver with a growing menagerie of cats and a few probable ghosts. The Afterlife of Mal Caldera (Titan Books, 2024) is their debut book and was an Amazon Editor’s Pick for Best Science Fiction and Fantasy. You can find them on Instagram and (for better or worse) on Tumblr @nadireedperez.

Tiffany Pham

Go to Author Page

Tiffany Pham is is the CEO of Mogul, a company that supports diverse individuals & organizations to achieve their goals & cultivate meaningful success. A coder, she developed the first version of Mogul, now reaching millions of people across 196 countries and 30,470 cities. She is a graduate of Yale University and Harvard Business School, and previously worked for Goldman Sachs, Credit Suisse, HBO, and CBS. Tiffany was named one of Forbes’ “30 Under 30” in Media, and is the recipient of dozens of awards and commendations for her work. Her debut book, You Are a Mogul: How to Do the Impossible, Do It Yourself, and Do It Now was an instant national bestseller, and was followed up by a companion for younger readers: Girl Mogul: Dream. Do It. Change the World. Her latest workshop Building Trust to Build a Network is exclusively available on the Audible for Business platform. She was a judge on the TLC show Girl Starter, and co-host of Positive Pushback, and has produced films that have screened in festivals around the world. She speaks on a regular basis at the United Nations to present gender policy recommendations, as well as MSNBC, Bloomberg, Viacom, Microsoft, AOL, Prudential, SXSW, Harvard Business School, Wharton Business School, Scripps Research Institute, Paris, Berlin, Vienna, Panama & Dubai. She lives in New York City.

Charles Piller

Go to Author Page

Charles Piller is an author and journalist who writes investigative stories for Science. He previously worked as an investigative journalist for STAT, the Los Angeles Times, and The Sacramento Bee, and has reported on public health, biological warfare, infectious disease outbreaks, and other topics from the United States, Africa, Asia, Europe, and Central America. Charles has won numerous journalism honors, including the 2016 AAAS/Kavli Science Journalism Award, the American Society of Tropical Medicine and Hygiene Communications Award, the National Council on Crime and Delinquency PASS Award, and the First Amendment Coalition Free Speech & Open Government Award, and the 2023 and 2024 National Institute of Health Care Management Trade Journalism Award. He has authored two investigative books about science and has testified before the U.S. Senate about his investigations of government compliance with the Freedom of Information Act, and workplace electronic surveillance. He was a founding board member of the Center for Public Integrity. His latest book, Doctored: Fraud, Arrogance, and Tragedy in the Quest to Cure Alzheimer’s, was published by One Signal/Simon & Schuster in 2025 and was named one of The Economist‘s Top Books of the Year.

Shivaun Plozza

Go to Author Page

Shivaun Plozza is an award-winning author of books for children and young adults. Her YA novels include Frankie, Tin Heart, and The Worst Perfect Moment. She is also the author of several middle-grade novels, including The Boy, the Wolf, and the Stars and A Reluctant Witch’s Guide to Magic, published by Clarion Books, and Meet Me at the Moon Tree, Summer of Shipwrecks, and Running in Circles, published by University of Queensland Press. Her books have been CBCA Notable Books and won the Davitt Award and received commendation from the Victorian Premier’s Literary Award, and have received multiple starred reviews. She is a frequent contributor to anthologies, and her short work has also appeared in Above Water, Vivid, and The Victorian Writer. She lives in Melbourne.

Aden Polydoros

Go to Author Page

Aden Polydoros is an award-winning author of dark fantasy and gothic horror novels. Their next YA novel, If I Can’t Have You, will be published by Page Street in 2027 and their queer adult horror novel, You Should Have Been Home By Now, will be published by Titan Books in 2027. In addition to receiving starred reviews from School Library Journal, Publishers Weekly, Bookpage, and Booklist, their novel, The City Beautiful, won the Sydney Taylor Book Award and was a finalist for the Lambda Award, the National Jewish Book Award, and the 2022 World Fantasy Award. A sequel, The City of Slaughter, will be released in June 2026. They are also the author of Bone Weaver and Wrath Becomes Her, which was a Sydney Taylor Honor Book. Their short fiction appeared in The Gathering Dark: An Anthology of Folk Horror. Their debut middle grade fantasy, Rings of Solomon, was published in 2023 and was followed by a sequel, Nightmares in Paradise, in 2024.

Alissa Quart

Go to Author Page

Alissa Quart is a journalist, poet, and the author of four books of nonfiction: Branded: The Buying and Selling of Teenagers, Hothouse Kids: The Dilemma of the Gifted Child, Republic of Outsiders: The Power of Amateurs, Dreamers, and Rebels, Squeezed: Why Our Families Can’t Afford America, and Bootstrapped: Liberating Ourselves from the American Dream. She is the Executive Director of the Economic Hardship Reporting Project, a journalism non-profit devoted to reporting on inequality. Her work has appeared in The New York Times, The Atlantic, The New York Review of Books, and The Guardian, among many other publications. She is also the author of two published collections of poetry, Monetized and Thoughts and Prayers, and her poems have been published by Granta, The London Review of Books, and The Nation. She executive produced the Emmy award-winning documentary “Jackson,” which recounts how the radical pro-life movement pressures and recruits low-income pregnant women and keeps them mired in poverty. Her next work of nonfiction, Terra Infirma, will be published by Pantheon. She lives with her husband and daughter in Brooklyn, New York.

James Raney

Go to Author Page

James Raney is the author of numerous books for young readers, including the Jim Morgan series, a two-time Foreword Reviews Books of the Year Finalist for Juvenile Fiction, and winner of the Writer’s Digest Book Award for YA/Middle Grade Fiction. He lives in California, where he frequently tours schools and bookstores to talk about his work.

Joan Reardon

Go to Author Page

Joan Reardon is an author and attorney from Youngstown, Ohio. Her love for Midwestern history and folklore inspired her debut novel The Grimsbane Family Witch Hunters, which was published by Simon & Schuster in August 2024. Her second middle grade novel, Dad Rock Dragon Quest, was published in June 2025. Joan holds a BA in history from The Ohio State University and earned her law degree from Case Western Reserve University School of Law. When she’s not writing, Joan enjoys going on adventures with her wonderful husband, Ben, and spending time with her incredibly large, incredibly loving family.

Amy Richards

Go to Author Page

Amy Richards is an author, speaker, producer and third-wave feminist leader. She is the author of Manifesta: Young Women, Feminism, and the Future (co-authored with Jennifer Baumgarder), Grassroots: A Field Guide for Feminist Activism (co-authored with Jennifer Baumgardner) and Opting In: Having a Child Without Losing Yourself. An advisor for the media brand MAKERS, she is also the author of We Are Makers: Real Women and Girls Shaping Our World and its companion Make Your Mark: A Journal for Capturing Big Dreams for young readers. With Jennifer Baumgardner, she is co-founder of Soapbox Inc: Speakers Who Speak Out, a feminist speaker’s agency representing a diverse list of activists and thought leaders, including Gloria Steinem, Jill Soloway, and Kathy Najimy. Recently, she produced the Emmy-nominated series WOMAN for Viceland and curated a series of talks to accompany Annie Leibowitz’s traveling exhibit, WOMEN, as well as acted as consulting producer for HBO’s Gloria Steinem: In Her Own Words and PBS’s MAKERS: Women Making America. Her media appearances include The O’Reilly Factor, Oprah, Talk of the Nation, New York One, and CNN. She co-founded the Third Wave Foundation, and has run Ask Amy, Feminist.com’s online advice column since 1995. She lives in New York City. 

Tohby Riddle

Go to Author Page Go to Portfolio

Nominated for the 2022 Hans Christian Andersen Medal, Tohby Riddle is the multi-award-winning creator of numerous picture books and illustrated books, which have been widely recognised in Australia and overseas for their quality and originality. This includes one of the world’s greatest living writers Haruki Murakami translating Tohby’s book My Uncle’s Donkey into Japanese after discovering it in a Melbourne bookshop in 2018. Other books include The Great Escape from City Zoo, The Singing Hat, Nobody Owns the Moon and most recently The Astronaut’s Cat. Tohby also illustrated the Word Spy series with Ursula Dubosarsky (known as The Word Snoop in the US).

Kelly Robinson

Go to Author Page

Kelly Robinson is a two-time Bram Stoker Award finalist and the recipient of Writer of the Year in the Rondo Hatton Classic Horror Awards. She has particular interests in horror, film, and excavating forgotten aspects of history, which she has written about in outlets like The Guardian, Smithsonian, Mental Floss, Scary Monsters, and Atlas Obscura. Her eclectic obsessions have led to lecturing at the Library of Congress on the topic of gorillas in film, being interviewed in a BBC documentary about snails, and writing sea shanties for McSweeney’s.

Shauna Robinson

Go to Author Page

Shauna Robinson is the author of Must Love Books, The Banned Bookshop of Maggie Banks, The Townsend Family Recipe for Disaster and Lauryn Harper Falls Apart. Her novels have been selected for the Indie Next List and recognized as Best Books of the Month by Amazon and LibraryReads. She received her Bachelor’s degree in English from UC Berkeley and lives in Virginia with her husband and their sleepy greyhound.

Kelsey Rodkey

Go to Author Page

Kelsey Rodkey is the author of the young adult novels Last Chance Books, A Disaster in Three Acts, Plus One, and Class Act, and is currently working on her debut adult novel. She is a banter enthusiast always in search of scary movies, delicious carbs, and her next five-star read. She once helped a British film crew make a documentary about creative dog grooming and has a terrible fear of waterslides. She lives in Central Pennsylvania with her partner and their cats, Cheese and Tux.

David Rose

Go to Author Page

David Rose is an investigative journalist and author whose work has appeared in dozens of publications, including The Observer and Vanity Fair. His books of nonfiction include In the Name of the Law: The Collapse of Criminal Justice, Regions of the Heart: The Triumph and Tragedy of Alison Hargreaves, Guantanamo: America’s War on Human Rights, and The Big Eddy Club: The Stocking Stranglings and Southern Justice. He is also the author of a novel, the thriller Taking Morgan. An avid amateur caver and mountaineer, his next project is The World Beneath the World, a nonfiction book about caving that combines history, reporting, and memoir. He lives in Oxford with his family.

Rabbi Danya Ruttenberg

Go to Author Page

Rabbi Danya Ruttenberg is an award-winning author, educator, and rabbi. Since her ordination from the Ziegler School of Rabbinic Studies in 2008, she has been named by Newsweek and The Daily Beast as a “rabbi to watch,” and called one of the top 50 most influential female rabbis by Forward. She has written for The New York Times, The Atlantic, Salon, Time, is a regular contributor to The Washington Post and The Forward, and has made appearances on GMA3, NPR, CBC, Al Jazeera America, and elsewhere. Her next book, Life is a Sacred Text, was sold at auction and will be published by St. Martin’s Press in 2027. Her previous publications are On Repentance And Repair: Making Amends in an Unapologetic World, winner of the National Jewish Book Award for Contemporary Jewish Life and Practice and an ALA Sophie Brody Honor Book, and Mend the World: Spiritual Tools for Healing, Repair, and Justice, an original audiobook from Sounds True. Her previous titles include Nurture the Wow: Finding Spirituality in the Frustration, Boredom, Tears, Poop, Desperation, Wonder, and Radical Amazement of Parenting (a National Jewish Book Award finalist), Surprised by God: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love Religion, and The Passionate Torah: Sex and Judaism. She is the founder of LifeIsASacredText.com and lives in Chicago with her family.

O.O. Sangoyomi 

Go to Author Page

O.O. Sangoyomi is a Nigerian American author with a penchant for African mythology and history. During a childhood of constantly moving around within the US, she found an anchored home in the fictional worlds of books. Sangoyomi is a recent graduate of Princeton University, where she studied English and African American Studies. Her debut novel, Masquerade, an Indie Next Pick and recipient of three starred reviews, was published by Macmillan/Forge in July 2024.

Jason Schmidt

Go to Author Page

Jason Schmidt is the author of a memoir, A List of Things that Didn’t Kill Me, and contributed to the YA anthology I See Reality: Twelve Short Stories About Real Life. His work has appeared in Jeopardy Magazine, Cranky Literary Journal, and ZYZZYVA. He holds degrees in creative writing and law from the University of Washington. Born in Oregon, he currently lives in Washington, D.C.

Jeremy Seal

Go to Author Page

Jeremy Seal is an award-winning travel writer, journalist, reviewer, and tour leader. He is the author of five books of nonfiction: A Fez of the Heart: Travels around Turkey in Search of a Hat, The Snakebite Survivors’ Club: Travels Among Serpents, Treachery at Sharpnose Point: Unraveling the Mystery of the Caledonia’s Final Voyage, Nicholas: The Epic Journey from Saint to Santa Claus, and Meander: East to West, Indirectly, Along a Turkish River. He has written about and traveled extensively in Turkey, and runs numerous tours and holidays there annually. He lives in Bath with his family.

Billy Sharff & Aaron Sharff

Go to Author Page

Brothers Billy Sharff and Aaron Sharff have been writing together for over 15 years. They co-wrote the 2013 feature film Before I Sleep and collaborated on Billy’s published works including When Santa Came to Stay, illustrated by Eda Kaban (Penguin Dial, 2022), Joan in the Cone, illustrated by Hala Tahboub (Penguin Dial, 2025), Robot’s First Snow, illustrated by Hannah Abbo (Sourcebooks, 2025), and Rock & Pebble (Penguin Dial, 2027). They began co-authoring children’s books in 2024. Billy lives in the woods of New Hampshire and Aaron lives in coastal Maine.

Melanie Sheppard

Go to Author Page

Melanie Sheppard is a children’s book author and former school librarian. She holds a Bachelor’s degree in English and a Master’s degree in Library & Information Science. When she’s not writing, Melanie enjoys cozy hobbies and exploring the outdoors. She resides in the Midwest with her family.

Delia Sherman

Go to Author Page

Delia Sherman is a critically-acclaimed and award-winning author of fiction for adults and young readers. Her novel The Porcelain Dove was a New York Times Notable Book and won the Mythopoeic Fantasy Award, and her young adult novel, The Freedom Maze, won the Andre Norton Award for YA Science Fiction and Fantasy. Her other works include Changeling, The Magic Mirror of the Mermaid Queen, The Evil Wizard Smallbone, and the collection Young Woman in a Garden and Other Stories. She is a frequent lecturer and teacher, and has taught writing at the Clarion Science Fiction Writer’s Workshop, among many others. Born in Japan, she lives in New York City with her wife, author Ellen Kushner. 

Alan Silberberg

Go to Author Page

Alan Silberberg is an award-winning author, cartoonist, and children’s TV creator who has worked with Nickelodeon and Disney. He is the author and illustrator of three middle grade novels: Pond Scum, Milo: Stick Notes & Brain Freeze (winner of the SCBWI’s Sid Fleishman Humor Award), and The Awesome, Almost 100% True Adventures of Matt & Craz. He is the author of multiple Jewish holiday and food-themed books published by Viking: the picture books Meet the Latkes, Meet the Matzah, Meet the Hamantaschen, The Bagel Who Wanted Everything, and The Falafel Who Felt Awfuk; and the board books Latke’s First Hanukkah and P is for Pastrami. He will publish Hanukkah Party Time, a new original picture book with PJ Publishing, in 2027. He lives with his family in Montreal.

Josh Similien

Go to Author Page Go to Portfolio

Josh Similien is an illustrator and visual development artist. Before pursuing his lifelong passion for art, Josh served in the U.S. military, with tours in Afghanistan and Iraq as a government contractor. He is passionate about visual storytelling – growing up he loved newspaper cartoons and filled drawers with clippings of comic strips and his own drawings, and he later became mesmerized by the movement of drawings in animation. Outside of animation and cartooning Josh enjoys movies, tv, music, and dance. He is currently studying Character Animation at the California Institute of the Arts.

Elsa Sjunneson

Go to Author Page

Elsa Sjunneson is a Deafblind author and editor living in Seattle, Washington. Her fiction and nonfiction writing has been praised as “eloquence and activism in lockstep” and has been published in dozens of venues around the world. She has been a Hugo Award finalist nine times, and has won Hugo, Aurora, and BFA awards for her editorial work. When she isn’t writing, Sjunneson works to dismantle structural ableism and rebuild community support for disabled people everywhere. Her debut memoir, Being Seen: One Deafblind Woman’s Fight to End Ableism, released in October 2021 from Simon & Schuster. Her next book, Dear Blind Lady: Disability Advice You Didn’t Know You Needed, will be published by Sasquatch Books in October 2026.

Kelley Skovron

Go to Author Page

Kelley Skovron is the author of over a dozen books for adults and young readers. Her novels for adults include the Empire of Storms trilogy (Hope and Red, Bane and Shadow, Blood and Tempest) which has been published in nine foreign territories, and the Goddess War trilogy (The Ranger of Marzanna, The Queen of Izmoroz, The Wizard of Eventide). Her middle grade and young adult novels include the G.I. Joe Classified series, The Hacker’s Key, The Ghost of Drowned Meadow, Man Made Boy, and This Broken Wondrous World. Her next middle grade horror, No Filter, was published in February 2024 by Scholastic. Kelley’s short fiction for young readers has appeared in anthologies such as Grim and Summer Days and Summer Nights, and her work has been published in ChiZine and Baen’s Universe, among others. As J.S. Kelley, she is the author of Gutter Mage. She and her cat currently live in a bread factory in Ohio.

Patrick Smith

Go to Author Page

Patrick Smith is an airline pilot, air travel blogger, and author. His “Ask the Pilot” column was a fixture in Salon for over ten years. He currently hosts the popular site Ask the Pilot, which covers all aspects of air travel, and is the author of the bestselling book Cockpit Confidential: Everything You Need to Know About Air Travel: Questions, Answers, and Reflections. He has appeared on over 200 radio and television outlets, and his work is frequently cited in numerous publications worldwide. An avid traveler who has visited more than eighty countries, he currently lives in Somerville, Massachusetts.

Leah Stecher

Go to Author Page

Leah Stecher was born and raised in Southern California and currently lives in coastal Maine. By day, she edits policy papers for an environmental nonprofit; by night, she writes middle grade fiction. She has published three middle grade novels with Bloomsbury: The Things We Miss, an Indie Next Pick and ALA Notable Book that received three starred reviews; A Field Guide to Broken Promises, and the forthcoming Hello, Whoever You Are. She has strong opinions on tea blends, chocolate chip cookie recipes, and action movies.

T.J. Stiles

Go to Author Page

T.J. Stiles is an award-winning historian and author. His most recent book, Custer’s Trials: A Life on the Frontier of a New America won the Pulitzer Prize for History. He is also the author of The First Tycoon: The Epic Life of Cornelius Vanderbilt, a New York Times bestseller which received the Pulitzer Prize for Biography and the National Book Award for Nonfiction, and Jesse James: Last Rebel of the Civil War, winner of the Ambassador Book Award and the Peter Seaborg Award for Civil War Scholarship. He is a current NEH Public Scholar and former Guggenheim Fellow, and has served on the faculty for the World Economic Forum and on the board of the Authors Guild, the Organization of American Historians, and the Society of American Historians. He lives in Berkeley, California with his wife and children, where he is working on a new biography of Theodore Roosevelt.

Sally Susman

Go to Author Page

Sally Susman is the Executive Vice President and Chief Corporate Affairs Officer at Pfizer, leading their external engagement, which includes global policy, communications, government relations, corporate responsibility, investor relations, and the Chief Patient Office. She is vice chair of The Pfizer Foundation. Her debut book, Breaking Through, was published by Harvard Business Review Press and was a Wall Street Journal bestseller. Before joining Pfizer in 2007, Sally held several senior communications and government positions at Estée Lauder Companies and the American Express Company. Before that she spent eight years on Capitol Hill focused on international trade issues and was Deputy Assistant Secretary for Legislative Affairs in the Clinton Administration. Sally serves as co-chair of The International Rescue Committee, one of the world’s largest humanitarian aid organizations, and is on the board of WPP, the U.K.-based global advertising and marketing company. She is a member of the Council on Foreign Relations, and a frequent commentator in the media. Sally was named a 2019 Top Voice on LinkedIn and was the recipient of an Outstanding Individual Achievement SABRE in 2022. A graduate of Connecticut College with a B.A. in government, Sally studied at the London School of Economics. She lives in Manhattan and Sag Harbor with her wife Robin Canter.

Jesse Q Sutanto

Go to Author Page

Jesse Q Sutanto is a bestselling and award-winning author of books for adults and young readers. She is the author of the Dial A series, comprised of Dial A for Aunties, Four Aunties and a Wedding, and The Good, the Bad, and the Aunties. Dial A for Aunties was an instant IndieBound bestseller, an Amazon Best Book and Amazon Editors’ Choice, and won the UK’s Comedy Women in Print prize. She is the author of the Theo Tan middle grade duology, the contemporary YA novels Well, That Was Unexpected and Didn’t See That Coming; the adult thrillers I’m Not Done With You Yet and You Will Never Be Me; the cozy mysteries Vera Wong’s Unsolicited Advice for Murderers (a USA Today bestseller and 2024 Edgar Award winner) and its sequel Vera Wong’s Guide to Snooping (on a Dead Man); Worth Fighting For (a USA Today bestseller and part of Disney’s Meant to Be series), Next Time Will Be Our Turn, Read Between the Lies (a Mindy’s Book Studio release), and Ms. Mebel Goes Back to the Chopping Block. Her forthcoming titles include An Edge Sharp Enough, her debut dark fantasy novel, a second suspense novel forthcoming from Amazon Publishing, and the next two installments of the Vera Wong series. She is also the author of the YA thrillers The Obsession andThe New Girl. She received her Masters from the University of Oxford. She grew up in Indonesia and Singapore and currently lives in Jakarta with her husband and two daughters.

Ella Synclair

Go to Author Page

Ella Synclair is a former attorney who now writes romance and romantic comedies. She is an alum of Pitch Wars and in addition to writing, she is an artist whose work has been featured in juried art shows and galleries. Her debut novel, Lucky Break, will be released by Pen & Leaf Press in 2026. She lives with her husband and three children in Chicago.

Marianne Szegedy-Maszak

Go to Author Page

Marianne Szegedy-Maszak is an author and award-winning journalist whose work has appeared in The New York Times Magazine, Esquire, The New Republic, Newsweek, the Los Angeles Times, and Psychology Today. Currently the editorial operations director in the Washington, D.C. bureau of Mother Jones, she previously worked for the New York Post, Congressional Quarterly, U.S. News & World Report and was a professor of journalism at American University. She is the accomplished ghostwriter of three high profile books and the author of the acclaimed memoir I Kiss Your Hands Many Times: Hearts, Souls and Wars in Hungary.

Kate & Danny Tamberelli

Go to Author Page

Kate & Danny Tamberelli are the authors of two romcoms for adults: The First Date Prophecy (2023) and The Road Trip Rewind (2024), published by Kensington. Kate—as Katelyn Detweiler—is a literary agent and the author of the YA novels Immaculate, Transcendent, The Undoing of Thistle Tate, and The People We Choose. Danny is an actor, musician, and comedian who rose to fame on Nickelodeon as the star of TV shows like The Adventures of Pete & Pete, All That, and Figure It Out. He currently hosts The Adventures of Danny & Mike, a podcast with his Pete & Pete co-star Mike Maronna, the live comedy show Nostalgia Personified, and is the bassist for the band Jounce. Kate and Danny live in Brooklyn with their children.

Lian Tanner

Go to Author Page

Lian Tanner is a playwright and award-winning author of books for young readers, including the Keepers series and the Icebreaker series. Museum of Thieves and City of Lies, the first two books of the Keepers series, both won the Aurealis Award for Children’s Fiction. Her work is available in seven foreign languages. Prior to working as a writer, she was a professional actor and freelance journalist. She lives in Tasmania.

Polo Tate

Go to Author Page

Polo Tate is an actor, artist, comedian, and author of Deep Dark Blue: My Story of Surviving Sexual Assault in the Military, about her experience at the United States Air Force Academy. After leaving the academy, she received her B.A. from the University of Notre Dame. She has made media appearances on Megyn Kelly TODAY, spoken at TEDx, and is a regular contributor to Mogul. She is Director of the R.E. Olds Foundation, the oldest family/private foundation in the state of Michigan, named for her great-grandfather Ransom Eli Olds, inventor of the Oldsmobile. As an actor and comedian, she regularly performs at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre, Broadway Comedy Club, Reckless, and the PIT. She lives in New York City.

They Might Be Giants

Go to Author Page

They Might Be Giants is an American alternative music group from Brooklyn, New York. The group was founded by John Flansburgh and John Linnell, who released their first album in 1986. Throughout their 35+ year career, the band has racked up several charting albums and singles, as well as two Grammy awards—one in 2002 for their song “Boss Of Me”, (the theme song for Malcolm in the Middle) and the other in 2009 for their children’s album Here Come The 123s. They continue to record alternative albums while branching out into the realm of children’s music, and have sold over 4 million records in total.

Alicia Thompson

Go to Author Page

Alicia Thompson is a reader, writer, and lover of baseball. She is the USA Today bestselling author of adult contemporary romances published by Berkley, including but not limited to Love in the Time of Serial Killers, With Love, from Cold World, and The Art of Catching Feelings. She lives in Central Florida with her husband, two kids, and two cats named Pickles and Luna. 

Addie Thorley

Go to Author Page

Addie Thorley is a New York Times and USA Today bestselling author of several novels for young adults, including An Affair of Poisons, which was chosen as a Barnes and Noble “Discover Great New Writers” selection and was a YALSA Best Fiction for Young Adults Nominee and the Night Spinner duology, comprised of Night Spinner and Sky Breaker. Her latest YA novel, Burn the Kingdom Down was published by Sourcebooks Fire in 2026 and was an instant New York Times and USA Today bestseller. Before becoming a full-time writer, she graduated with a degree in journalism from the University of Utah. She currently lives in New Jersey with her husband and children.

Jennifer Thorne

Go to Author Page

Jennifer Thorne is the British-American author of Lute and Diavola (a USA Today bestseller and Goodreads Choice Award Finalist), published by Tor/Nightfire. Her next novel, Newbourne Park, will be published by Putnam. With Lee Kelly, she is co-author of The Antiquity Affair (2023) and The Starlets (2024), published by HarperMuse. As Jenn Marie Thorne, she is the author of the YA novels The Wrong Side of Right, The Inside of Out, and Night Music, and her short fiction appeared in the YA anthology Battle of the Bands. She is also the author of the picture book Construction Zoo. She received her BFA in Drama at NYU-Tisch, and lives in Gloucestershire, England with her husband and two sons.

Michele Thornton

Go to Author Page

Michele Thornton is a young adult author, currently working on her debut novel. She received her degree in English with a focus on creative writing, and later worked in marketing. She grew up in New England, and currently lives in Oregon.

Julie Tollefson

Go to Author Page

Julie Tollefson is a writer and editor whose short fiction has appeared in several anthologies, including Life is Short and Then You Die: First Encounters with Murder from Mystery Writers of America, and in Alfred Hitchcock Mystery Magazine, which featured her story “Leah” as its November/December 2018 cover story. In 2016, she received the Hugh Holton Award for unpublished crime fiction manuscripts, given annually by the Mystery Writers of America Midwest Chapter. Julie grew up in the sand hills of Southwest Kansas, where she landed her first paying writing gig just out of high school, investigating such hard-hitting stories as “Do blondes really have more fun?” for her hometown newspaper. She has a journalism degree from the University of Kansas and a master’s of library and information management from Emporia State University. She and her husband live in northeast Kansas, where she is at work on her first novel. 

Trang Thanh Tran

Go to Author Page

Trang Thanh Tran is a queer Vietnamese American writer telling all stories scary, otherworldly, and emotional for young adults and adults. Their YA debut novel, She Is a Haunting, was an instant New York Times and IndieBound bestseller, an IndieNext pick, and was selected by Target and Barnes & Noble as a YA Book Club pick. It was named a finalist for the 2024 William C. Morris YA Debut Award and won the Bram Stoker Award for Superior Achievement in a Young Adult Novel. They Bloom at Night, released in March 2025 was an instant IndieBound bestseller, an Indie Next Pick, winner of the ABA’s Indie Book Award, shortlisted for the Barnes & Noble Children’s & YA Book Award, and received two starred reviews. Their next novel The Summoning of Tess Pham, is slated for release in 2027. They are an alum of the Writing Barn’s Rainbow Weekend 2020 and Tin House’s YA Fiction Workshop in 2021. They currently live in the South with their family and two mischievous cats.

Taleen Voskuni

Go to Author Page

Taleen Voskuni is a USA Today bestselling and award-winning writer who grew up in the Bay Area Armenian diaspora. She is the author of three romantic comedies, Sorry, Bro, Lavash at First Sight, and Our Ex’s Wedding, published by Berkley. She graduated from UC Berkeley with a BA in English and currently lives in San Francisco, working in tech. Other than a newfound obsession with writing rom-coms, she spends her free time cultivating her kids, her garden, and her dark chocolate addiction. Sorry, Bro is the winner of the 2023 Golden Poppy award for best romance.

Jonathan Weiler

Go to Author Page

Jonathan Weiler is a professor and author of four books: Authoritarianism and Polarization in American Politics (winner of the Philip Converse Award from the American Political Science Association), Prius or Pickup?: How the Answers to Four Simple Questions Explain America’s Great Divide (co-authored with Marc Hetherington), Human Rights in Russia: A Darker Side of Reform and Divorce: A Love Story. He received his PhD in political science from UNC Chapel Hill, where he is Director of Undergraduate Studies, Associate Chair of Global Studies, and teaches courses on economic globalization, sports and globalization, and human rights.

Scott Westerfeld

Go to Author Page

Scott Westerfeld is the #1 New York Times bestselling author of over 20 books for adults and young readers, including the Uglies and Impostors series, the Leviathan trilogy, the Midnighters trilogy, the New York trilogy, the Zeroes series (with Margo Lanagan and Deborah Biancotti), the Spill Zone graphic novel duology, the novel Afterworlds, and the first book in the Horizon series for young readers. His debut picture book, Before You Were Here, was published by Roaring Brook. His books have sold over five million copies worldwide, and his work has been translated into over 32 languages. The highly anticipated film adaptation of Uglies, starring Joey King and Laverne Cox, is now streaming on Netflix and was the #1 movie on the platform in the US and over 60 countries. An anime adaptation of Leviathan was released on Netflix in 2025. He has won and been nominated for numerous awards, including the Locus, the Nebula, and the Aurealis. He and his wife, author Justine Larbalestier, will publish their jointly-authored adult debut, The Mortons, in 2026.

Ruth Whiting

Go to Author Page

Ruth Whiting is an artist and maker with a diverse body of work, including illustration, large-scale sculptural installations, and classic oil paintings. Her debut trilogy of picture books about a character named Lonely Bird was acquired by Candlewick. The first title, Lonely Bird (2023), was a Junior Library Guild selection and was followed by Lonely Bird’s Dream (2024). She has exhibited in Europe, Australia and America. Born in England to Australian parents, Ruth now lives in Florida with her husband, the artist and kite designer Tim Elverston, and their son. As a child, Ruth wanted nothing more than to read books. Her love of stories fuels her work as an illustrator and author.

Lili Wilkinson

Go to Author Page

Lili Wilkinson is the author of over a dozen books for children and young adults, including the YA novels The Boundless Sublime, After the Lights Go Out, The Erasure Initiative, A Hunger of Thorns, Deep is the Fen, Unhallowed Halls, and Lavender and Hemlock. As L.M. Wilkinson, she is the author of Bravepaw quartet of middle grade novels illustrated by Lavanya Naidu. She helped establish InsideADog.com.au and The Inky Awards — Australia’s first national teen choice award — at the State Library of Victoria’s Centre for Youth Literature. She received her PhD in Creative Writing from the University of Melbourne, and lives in Melbourne with her husband and son.

Sean Williams

Go to Author Page

Sean Williams is the author of over 100 published short stories and 40 novels for children and adults, including Impossible Music, the Twinmaker trilogy, and three books in the Star Wars: New Jedi Order series. His novelization, Star Wars: The Force Unleashed was a #1 New York Times bestseller. With Garth Nix, he is the co-author of the Troubletwisters series, the Have Sword, Will Travel series, and Spirit Animals: Blood Ties. His latest releases are Her Perilous Mansion and Honour Among Ghosts, a middle grade fantasy duology. Called “the premier Australian speculative fiction writer of the age” by Aurealis magazine, he has received multiple Aurealis and Ditmar Awards, was nominated for the Philip K. Dick Award, and received an Antarctica Arts Fellowship. He lives in Adelaide, where he is currently Professor of Creative Writing at Flinders University.

Fiona Wood

Go to Author Page

Fiona Wood is the award-winning author of four novels of young adult fiction, including Six Impossible Things, Wildlife, Cloudwish, and Take Three Girls, co-written with Simmone Howell and Cath Crowley. She is the author of two middle grade novels How to Spell Catastrophe (Pan Macmillan, 2022) and The Boy and the Dog Tree (University of Queensland Press, 2026). She has received three Children’s Book Council of Australia Book of the Year Awards and has been published internationally. Before becoming an author, she was a television scriptwriter, worked in marketing and arts management, and was a freelance journalist. She lives with her husband in Melbourne, Australia.

Colin Woodard

Go to Author Page

Colin Woodard is an award-winning journalist and the author of seven books: Ocean’s End: Travels Through Endangered Seas; The Lobster Coast: Rebels, Rusticators, and the Struggle for a Forgotten Frontier; The Republic of Pirates: Being the True and Surprising Story of the Caribbean Pirates and the Man Who Brought Them Down (a New York Times bestseller and the basis for the NBC series Crossbones, starring John Malkovich); American Nations: A History of the Eleven Rival Regional Cultures of North America (a Wall Street Journal bestseller and named a Best Book by The New Republic and The Globalist); American Character: A History of the Epic Struggle Between Individual Liberty and the Common Good; and Union: The Struggle to Forge the Story of United States Nationhood. His latest book, Nations Apart: How Clashing Regional Cultures Shattered America was published by Viking in November 2025. He is currently Director of Nationhood Lab at the Pell Center for International Relations and Public Policy at Salve Regina University, an interdisciplinary research, writing, testing and dissemination project focused on counteracting the authoritarian threat to American democracy and the centrifugal forces threatening the federation’s stability. He is also a POLITICO contributing editor. He is on sabbatical from his position as State and National Affairs Writer for the Portland Press Herald/Maine Sunday Telegram, where he was a Pulitzer Prize finalist and George Polk Award winner for investigative reporting. He lives with his family in Maine.

Geraldine Woods

Go to Author Page

Geraldine Woods is a former educator and the author of more than 50 books, including English Grammar for Dummies, which has been translated into ten languages, and Webster’s New World Punctuation: Simplified and Applied. She taught for three decades at the prestigious Horace Mann School, where she created dozens of curricular units, many for grammar instruction and writing that have been emulated by teachers across the country, and around the world. She continues to mentor and advise educators. She wrote dozens of other For Dummies books such as College Admission Essays for Dummies, and Research Papers for Dummies. She is the author of Independent Study That Works: Designing a Successful Program, 25 Great Sentences and How They Got That Way, and Sentence.: A Period to Period Guide to Building Better Readers and Writers, all from W.W. Norton. She lives in New York City, where she keeps a blog, Grammarian in the City, providing wry commentary on language trends and the pretentious or incomprehensible signs she observes on her walks. 

Andi Zeisler

Go to Author Page

Andi Zeisler is an author and co-founder of Bitch Media, a nonprofit feminist media organization. Her work has appeared in numerous periodicals and newspapers, including Ms., Mother Jones, BUST, the San Francisco Chronicles, and the Women’s Review of Books. A former pop music columnist for the SF Weekly and the East Bay Express, has since co-edited BitchFest: 10 Years of Cultural Criticism from the Pages of Bitch Magazine, authored two books: Feminism and Pop Culture and We Were Feminists Once: From Riot Grrrl to CoverGirl®, the Buying and Selling of a Political Movement, and contributed to a number of anthologies. She lives in Portland.

Angela Zimmerman

Go to Author Page

Angela Zimmerman is an author, historian and professor of history at George Washington University. She received her M.Phil from the University of Cambridge, and her PhD from the University of California, San Diego. She is the author of a number of scholarly books and texts, including Alabama in Africa: Booker T. Washington, the German Empire, and the Globalization of the New South. She has received a Guggenheim Fellowship, and was most recently a fellow at the Institute for Advanced Study in Princeton. Her latest book, a new history of the Civil War focusing on immigrant radicals and slave rebels, is forthcoming from Knopf.

Jill Grinberg Literary Management LLC
